pages.text: 275-24  September 24, 2024  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   N.J.S.A.   26:3-22   authorizes   two   or   more   adjacent   municipalities   to   join   in  employing a health officer to provide   public health services ; and  WHEREAS,   N.J.S.A.   40A:65-4   of   the   Un i formed   Shared   Services   and   Consolidation  Act   authorizing   municipalities   to   enter   into   shared   services   agreement   to   either   provide   or  receive   any   services   that   each   local   unit   is   empowered   to   provide   or   receive   within   its   own  jurisdiction; and  WHEREAS,   on   January   24,   2023   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   adopted  Resolution   27-23,   whereby   the   Township   entered   into   a   Shared   Services   Agreement   with   the  Borough   of   Essex   Fells   (the   “ Borough ” )   whereby   the   Borough   may   utilize   the   Township ’ s   public  health officer to provide public health services;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Health   Officer   suddenly   passed   away   and   therefore,   the  Township   can   no   longer   provide   such   services   under   the   Shared   Services   Agreement   (the  “ Shared Service   Agreement ” ) ;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   terminate   the   Share   Service  Agreement   with   the   Borough   of   Essex   Fells   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized  to   provide a copy of this Resolution to the Borough ;   and be it further  RESOLVED   that   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:65-4(b)   of   the   Un i formed   Shared   Services  and   Consolidation   Act   the   Municipal   Clerk   shall   forward   a   copy   of   th is   Resoluton   to   the  Division   of   Local   Government   Services   in   the   Department   of   Community   Affairs   for  informationa l purposes; and be it further

RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   termination   shall   be   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with any applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September   24 , 2024

1  276-24  September 24, 202 4  RESOLUTION   AUTHORIZING TEMPORARY EASEMENT AGREEMENT  WITH   OWNER OF 9 NELSON COURT  WHEREAS ,   a   portion   of   the   Township ’ s   sanitary   sewer   main   runs   beneath   the  property   located   at   9   Nelson   Court,   West   Orange,   NJ   07052,   known   as   Block   168,   Lot   10.05;  and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Engineer   has   determined   that   there   are  immediate   improvements   that   need   to   be   initiated   for   the   install ation   of   a   pump   station   with   tank  and   grinder   pumps   including   tank,   check   valves,   electrical   work,   and   site   restoration   to   prevent  raw sewage from   the   sanitary sewer main   to discharge into the existing   residence ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   owner   of   the   Property   has   agreed   to   a   Deed   of   Temporary   Easement  and Temporary Easement Agreement attached herein as Exhibit   “ A ; ”   and  WHEREAS ,   the   owner   of   the   Property   has   further   agreed   to   maintain   and   repair   the  future   Pump   Station   improvements   including   all   its   components   as   necessary   to   protect   and  maintain a functional pump station; and  WHEREAS ,   the   scope   of   work   shall   include   the   installation   of   a   pump   station   that  includes   a   tank   with   pump,   check   valves,   covers,   conduit   and   associated   amenities   for   an  operational   station.   For   the   work   to   be   completed;   excavation   will   need   to   be   performed,   backfill  for   trench es ,   installation   of   electrical   connections   and   conduit,   restoration   and   topsoil   and  seeding all disturbed areas;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Engineer   has   deemed   these   repairs   necessary   to   protect   the  health and safety of residents located on   Nelson Court ; and

2  NOW,   BEING   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   Township   is   authorized   to   enter   into   the   Deed   of  Temporary Easement and Easement Agreement attached herein as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and   it is further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office  in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September   24 , 2024  I hereby certify funds are available from Account No.  ___  John Ditinyak ,   C hief Financial Officer

276-24  DEED OF   TEMPORARY   EASEMENT AND  TEMPORARY   EASEMENT AGREEMENT  This   Deed   of   Temporary   Easement   and   Temporary   Easement   Agreement   made  effective   as   of   September   ____ ,   202 4   BY   AND   BETWEEN   LINDA   FINNEY   residing   at   9  Nelson   Court ,   Township   of   West   Orange,   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New   Jersey,  hereinafter   referred   to   as   "GRANTOR,"   and   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   a  municipal   corporation   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   having   offices   located   at   66   Main   Street,   in  the   Township   of   West   Orange,   County   of   Essex,   and   State   of   New   Jersey,   hereinafter   referred  to as the "GRANTEE ”   or the   “ TOWNSHIP. ”  WITNESSETH  WHEREAS   the   Grantor   is   the   sole   owner   of   Tax   Block   168 ,   Lot   10.05   on   the   official  Tax   Map   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange ,   County   of   Essex ,   State   and   New   Jersey ,   also  known as   9 Nelson Court , West Orange, New Jersey   (hereinafter the   “ PREMISES ” ), and  WHEREAS   the   GRANTEE   has   determined   that   certain   immediate   improvements   and  other   repairs   are   needed   to   prevent   raw   sewage   from   a   portion   of   the   sanitary   sewer   main  beneath the PREMISES   to discharge into the residence ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   GRANTOR   is   willing   to   grant   to   the   GRANTEE   and   the   GRANTEE  is   willing   to   accept   an   easement   over   a   portion   of   said   premises   to   conduct   the   following  repairs   on   the   PREMISES :   installation   of   a   pump   station   that   includes   a   tank   with   pump,  check   valves,   covers,   conduit   and   associated   amenities   for   an   operational   station.   For   the  work   to   be   completed;   excavation   will   need   to   be   performed,   backfill   for   trench,   installation  of   electrical   connections   and   conduit,   restoration   and   topsoil   and   seeding   a ll   disturbed   area s ;  and  WHEREAS   the   GRANTEE   further   agrees   to   be   responsible   for   the   maintenance   and  repair   of   the   future   pump   station   improvements   and   its   components   at   their   premises   as  necessary to protect and maintain a functional pump station; and  WHEREAS   the   above-described   Temporary   Easement   being   as   shown   on   a   survey   to  be obtained by the   GRANT EE   as soon as the same is made available; and  The GRANTOR hereby covenants with the GRANTEE that:  a.   The   GRANTOR   has   good   and   lawful   right   to   convey   the   temporary   easement  reference herein.  b.   The   temporary   easement   is   free   and   clear   from   encumbrances   of   record   that  might interfere with the scope of work reference herein.  c.   The   GRANTEE   has   a   portion   of   the   sanitary   sewer   line   that   extends   across   a  portion of the GRANTOR ’ S property; and  d.   GRANTREE   will   provide   GRANTOR   at   least   48   hours ’   notice   prior   to   the  commence of the emergency repair;  e.   GRANTOR   does   hereby   expressly   permit   right   of   entry   and   temporary  easement   to   the   GRANTEE   for   the   purposes   of   conducting   the   installation   of

the   pump   station,   grinder   pumps   and   all   other   necessary   components   for   a  functional   station   with   the   intent   of   access   to   be   uninterrupted   and  unobstructed   right   of   entry,   under,   across   and   over   the   area   as   shown   in  “ Exhibit   A ”   consisting   of   the   right   to   excavate,   erect,   construct,   install,   use,  and impact property;  f.   GRANTOR,   its   successors,   and/or   assigns   shall   hold   harmless   GRANTEE,   its  successors   and/or   assigns   from   and   against   any   and   all   claim,   liabilities,  penalties,   costs   or   other   damages,   whether   foreseen   and   unforeseen,   arising  directly from the temporary easement reference herein.  g.   GRANTOR   agrees   that   if   any   disturbance   or   damage   is   caused   to   the   subject  property   as   a   result   of   the   GRANTEE ’ S   exercise   of   the   rights   and   privileges  afforded   to   it   under   this   instrument,   the   GRANTEE   shall   restore   the   affected  area as near as practicable to its original condition.  h.   GRANTOR   agrees,   acknowledges,   and   affirms   that   the   temporary   easement  hereunder granted shall be binding upon his heirs, successors, and assigns.  NOW   THEREFORE,   in   consideration   of   the   sum   of   One   ($1.00)   Dollars   and   other   good   and  valuable   consideration   paid   to   the   GRANTOR   by   the   G RANTEE ,   GRANTOR   does   hereby  grant   unto   the   GRANTEE ,   their   heirs,   successors   and   assigns   a   T emporary   Easement   to  conduct   emergency   repairs   to   the   sewer   and/or   stormwater   piping   located   on   the   PREMISES  in   the   area   to   be   prescribed   by   the   survey   to   be   provided   by   the   GRANTEE   to   the  GRANTOR .  IN   WITNESS   WHEREOF,   the   parties   have   hereunder   affixed   their   hands   and   seals  the day and year first above written.  ____________________________  LINDA FINNEY  ATTESTED  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ______________________________   ________________________(seal)  Karen   J.   Carnevale , Township Clerk   by : Susan McCartney, Mayor  STATE OF NEW JERSEY  COUNTY OF   ESSEX   ss:  I CERTIFY that on   September   ____, 20 2 4  Linda Finney   p ersonally came before me and stated to my satisfaction that   he   each person  e xecuted this instrument as his own act.  ______________________________
EXHIBIT A  [TO BE SUPPLIED]
EXHIBIT A

DEED OF TEMPORARY EASEMENT AND TEMPORARY EASEMENT AGREEMENT  This   Deed of Temporary Easement   and   Temporary   Easement   Agreement   made effective as of September   ____, 2024 BY AND BETWEEN LINDA FINNEY residing at 9 Nelson Court, Township of West Orange, County of Essex and State of New Jersey, hereinafter referred to as "GRANTOR," and THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, a municipal corporation of the State of New Jersey, having offices located at 66 Main Street, in the Township of West Orange, County of Essex, and State of New Jersey, hereinafter referred to as the "GRANTEE” or the “TOWNSHIP.”  WITNESSETH  WHEREAS   the Grantor is the sole owner of Tax Block 168, Lot 10.05 on the official Tax Map of the Township of West Orange, County of Essex, State and New Jersey, also known as 9 Nelson Court, West Orange, New Jersey (hereinafter the “PREMISES”), and  WHEREAS   the GRANTEE has determined that certain immediate improvements and other repairs are needed to prevent raw sewage from a portion of the sanitary sewer main beneath the PREMISES to discharge into the residence; and  WHEREAS , the GRANTOR is willing to grant to the GRANTEE and the GRANTEE is willing to accept an easement over a portion of said premises to conduct the following repairs on the PREMISES: installation of a pump station that includes a tank with pump, check valves, covers, conduit and associated amenities for an operational station. For the work to be completed; excavation will need to be performed, backfill for trench, installation of electrical connections and conduit, restoration and topsoil and seeding all disturbed areas; and  WHEREAS   the GRANTOR further agrees to be responsible for the maintenance and repair of the future pump station improvements and its components at their premises as necessary to protect and maintain a functional pump station; and  WHEREAS   the above-described Temporary Easement being as shown on a survey to be obtained by the GRANTEE as soon as the same is made available; and  The GRANTOR hereby covenants with the GRANTEE that:  a.   The GRANTOR has good and lawful right to convey the temporary easement reference herein.  b.   The temporary easement is free and clear from encumbrances of record that might interfere with the scope of work reference herein.  c.   The GRANTEE has a portion of the sanitary sewer line that extends across a portion of the GRANTOR’S property; and  d.   GRANTREE will provide GRANTOR at least 48 hours’ notice prior to the commence of the emergency repair;  e.   GRANTOR does hereby expressly permit right of entry and temporary easement to the GRANTEE for the purposes of conducting the installation of the pump station, grinder pumps and all other necessary components for a functional

station with the intent of access to be uninterrupted and unobstructed right of entry, under, across and over the area as shown in “Exhibit A” consisting of the right to excavate, erect, construct, install, use, and impact property;  f.   GRANTOR, its successors, and/or assigns shall hold harmless GRANTEE, its successors and/or assigns from and against any and all claim, liabilities, penalties, costs or other damages, whether foreseen and unforeseen, arising directly from the temporary easement reference herein.  g.   GRANTOR agrees that if any disturbance or damage is caused to the subject property as a result of the GRANTEE’S exercise of the rights and privileges afforded to it under this instrument, the GRANTEE shall restore the affected area as near as practicable to its original condition.  h.   GRANTOR agrees, acknowledges, and affirms that the temporary easement hereunder granted shall be binding upon his heirs, successors, and assigns.  NOW THEREFORE, in consideration of the sum of One ($1.00) Dollars and other good and valuable consideration paid to the GRANTOR by the GRANTEE, GRANTOR does hereby grant unto the GRANTEE, their heirs, successors and assigns a Temporary Easement to conduct emergency repairs to the sewer and/or stormwater piping located on the PREMISES in the area to be prescribed by the survey to be provided by the GRANTEE to the GRANTOR.  IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have hereunder affixed their hands and seals the day and year first above written.  ____________________________  LINDA FINNEY  ATTESTED  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ______________________________   ________________________(seal)  Karen Carnevale, Township Clerk   by: Susan McCartney, Mayor  STATE OF NEW JERSEY  COUNTY OF ESSEX   ss:  I CERTIFY that on September ____, 2024  Linda Finney personally came before me and stated to my satisfaction that he each person executed this instrument as his own act.  ______________________________
EXHIBIT A  [TO BE SUPPLIED]

pages.text: 281-24  September 24, 2024  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING CONTRACTS WITH CERTAIN APPROVED   COOPERTIVE  VENDORS FOR CONTRACTING UNITS PURSUANT TO   N.J.S.A. 40A:11-5(2)   FOR 2024  Whereas ,   pursuant to the provision made in the Local Public Contracts Law N.J.S.A.  40A:11-5(2) the Township as a   Local Government Unit, may without public advertising for  goods or services enter into contracts already secured by the United States of America, the State  of New Jersey, County or Municipality; and  Whereas , the Township of West Orange has the need on a timely basis to purchase goods  or services utilizing   Cooperative Contracts ; and  Whereas , the Township of West Orange intends to enter into contracts with the attached  Referenced   Cooperative   Contract Vendors through this resolution and properly executed  contracts, which shall be subject to all the conditions applicable to the current   Cooperative  contracts;  Now, Therefore, Be It Resolved , that the Township of West Orange   Council   authorizes  the Purchasing Agent to purchase certain goods or services from those approved   Cooperative  Contract Vendors on the attached list, pursuant to all conditions of the individual   Cooperative  contracts ; and  Be It Further Resolved , that the governing body of the Township of West Orange  pursuant to N.J.A.C. 5:30-5.5(b), the certification of available funds, shall either certify the full  maximum amount against the budget at the time the contract is awarded, or no contract amount  shall be chargeable or certified until such time as the goods or services are ordered or otherwise  called for prior to placing the order, and a certification of availability of funds is made by the  Chief Finance Officer; and  Be It Further Resolved , that the duration of the contracts between the Township of West  Orange and the Referenced State Contract Vendors shall be   through   December 31, 202 4 .  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: September 24, 2024  I hereby certify funds are available from   Various Accounts  ___  John Ditinyak, Chief Financial Officer

#14643418.1  2 82 -24  September   24 , 2024  RESOLUTION   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE  AUTHORIZING   THE   EXECUTION   OF   A   PURCHASE   AGREEMENT  AND   AMENDMENT   TO   REDEVELOPMENT   AGREEMENT   WITH  MDGA –   WEST ORA NGE DOWNTOWN REDEVELOPMENT, LLC .  WHEREAS ,   in   accordance   with   the   Local   Redevelopment   and   Housing   Law,   N.J.S.A.  40A:12A-1   el   seq.   (the   “ Redevelopment   Law ” ),   the   Township   has   designated   a   portion   of   its  downtown   area   generally   bounded   by   the   City   of   Orange   to   the   southeast,   Park   Avenue   to   the  southwest,   Llewellyn   Park   to   the   northwest,   and   Kling   Stre et   to   the   northeast,   and   containing  those   properties   identified   on   the   Official   Tax   Map   of   the   Township   as   Blocks   63,   64,   66,   115,  116,   116.02   and   portions   of   Blocks   89,   114   and   117   as   an   area   in   need   of   redevelopment  (collectively, the   “ Redevelopment Area ” ); and  WHEREAS,   in   accordance   with   the   Redevelopment   Law,   the   Township   enacted   a  redevelopment   plan   for   the   Redevelopment   Area   entitled   the   “ Downtown   Redevelopment   Plan  Historic West Orange ”   on February 9, 2003   (the   “ Original Redevelopment Plan ” ); and  WHEREAS,   the   Original   Redevelopment   Plan   has   been   amended   throughout   the   years  as   follows:   the   “ First   Amendment, ”   adopted   August   15,   2006,   the   “ Second   Amendment, ”  adopted   March   6,   2007,   the   “ Third   Amendment, ”   adopted   in   2010 ,   and   the   “ Fourth   Amendment ”  adopted   on   September   14,   2021   (the   Original   Redevelopment   Plan,   along   with   the   First  Amendment,   the   Second   Amendment,   the   Third   Amendment   and   the   Fourth   Amend ment  referred to collectively as the   “ Redevelopment Plan ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   Fourth   Amended   Redeve lopment   Plan   creates   a   new   overlay   zone   (the  Film   Services   Overlay   District   (the   “ FSOD ” ),   which   provides   for   film   production   activities  adjacent   to   the   location   of   the   world's   first   film   production   studio,   the   Black   Maria,   which   was  on the grounds of what is now the Thomas Edison National Historical Park; and  WHEREAS ,   the   FSOD   is   comprised   of   certain   properties   located   within   the  Redevelopment   Area   designated   as   Block   63,   Lots,   24,   25,   33.01,   33.02,   36,   37,   38,   39   and   41,  which   properties   are   located   east   of   Standish   Avenue   and   south   of   Lakeside   Avenue;   Block   64,  Lots   2.01,   2.03,   2.04,   3.02,   28,   30,   31,   32,   33,   34,   34.01,   38,42.02,   49   and   49.01,   which  properties   are   located   east   of   Ashland   Avenue,   south   of   Lakeside   Avenue   and   west   of   Standish  Avenue;   and   Block   115,   Lots   15,   16,   16.01   (a   portion   of   which   is   designated   as   Block   1302,   Lot  1   in   the   City   of   Orange),   17   and   51,   which   properties   are   located   north   of   Lakeside   Avenue,   west  of   Watchung   Avenue   and   south   of   Columbia   Street   (referred   to   collectively   as   the   “ FSOD  Properties ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   is   the   fee   title   owner   of   certain   real   propert ies   within   the  Redevelopment   Area   designated   as   Block   115,   Lots   15,   16,   and   51,   which   properties   are   located  north   of   Lakeside   Avenue,   west   of   Watchung   Avenue   and   south   of   Columbia   Street(the  “ Township Properties ” ); and

#14643418.1  WHEREAS ,   the   Redeveloper   has   expressed   an   interest   in   purchasing   and   redeveloping  the   Township   Propert ies   ( also   referred   to   collectively   as   the   “ Project   Site ” )   in   accordance   with  the   permitted   uses   set   forth   in   the   FSOD   under   the   Redevelopment   Plan   as   a   film   and   video  production campus   of between 150,000 to 300,000 square feet   ( the   “ Project ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   t he   Township   has   determined   that   Redeveloper   meets   all   necessary   criteria,  including   financial   capabilities,   experience,   expertise   and   project   concept   for   redevelopment   of  the   Project   Site   and,   as   a   result,   has   determined   to   engage   exclusively   in   negotiations   with  Redeveloper   for   the   purpose   of   entering   into   this   Redevelopment   Agreement   to   de signate  Redeveloper as the exclusive redeveloper of the   Project Site ; and  WHEREAS ,   in   order   to   implement   the   development,   financing,   construction,   operation  and   management   of   the   P roject,   the   Township   h as   determined   to   enter   into   the   Redevelopment  Agreement   with   Redeveloper,   in   the   form   attached   hereto   as   Exhibit   A ,   which   specifies   the   rights  and   responsibilities   of   the   Township ,   and   specifies   the   rights   and   responsibilities   of   Redeveloper  with respect to the Project; and  WHEREAS,   the   Redevelopment   Law,   N .J.S.A.   40A:12A-8(f),   authorizes   the   Township  to   arrange   or   contract   with   a   redeveloper   for   the   undertaking   of   any   project   or   redevelopment  work in an area designated as an area in need of redevelopment; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   has   been   in   discussions   with   Redeveloper   regarding  redevelopment   of   the   Redevelopment   Area   pursuant   to   the   FSOD   development   standards   under  the Redevelopment Plan; and  WHEREAS ,   on   December   5,   2023,   the   Township   adopted   Resolution   Number   405-23,  des ignating   Redeveloper   as   redevelo per   for   the   Project   Site   and   authorizing   the   execution   of   the  r edevelopment   a greement   (the   “ Redevelopment   Agreement ” ),   pursuant   to   which   Redeveloper  made   certain   payments   to   the   Township   and   conducted   further   due   diligence   of   the   Township  Properties, within the limited time frames specified in the Redevelopment Agreement; and  WHEREAS ,   Redeveloper   has   advanced   its   due   diligence   and   preliminary   work   with  respect   to   the   Project   to   the   point   that   the   Parties   are   prepared   to   enter   into   a   purchase   agreement  for   the   Township   Properties,   which   the   Parties   have   been   negotiating,   pursuant   to   the   material  terms set forth in the Redevelopment   Agreement; and  WHEREAS ,   in   connection   therewith,   the   initial   term   of   the   Redevelopment   Agreement  must   be   extended   and   certain   other   amendments   to   the   Redevelopment   Agreement   are  appropriate   in   connection   with   the   purchase   agreement   for   the   conveyance   of   the   Township  Properties; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   now   wishes   to   approve   and   execute   the   First   Amendment   to  Redevelopment   Agreem ent,   in   the   form   attached   hereto   as   Exhibit   A   (the   “ Amendment ” ) ,   setting  forth   the   specific   terms   upon   which   Redeveloper   will   purchase   the   Township   Properties ,   conduct  further   due   diligence   of   the   Township   Properties   within   the   time   frames   specified   in   the  Amendment,   extend   Redeveloper ’ s   status   as   the   designated   redeveloper   for   the   Project   Site   and

#14643418.1  make certain related amendments to the   Redevelopment Agreement .  NOW,   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of West Orange, as follow s:  Section 1.   MDGA – West   Orange   Downtown   Redevelopment,   LLC,   hereby   continues  to   be   designated   as   the   exclusive   redeveloper   of   the   Project   Site ,   for   and   upon   the   term   set   forth  in   the   Redevelopment   Agreement ,   as   amended   by   the   Amendment   in   the   form   attached   hereto   as  Exhibit A .  Section 2.   The   Mayor   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the   Amendment   to   the  Redevelopment Agreement in the form attached hereto as   Exhibit A .  Section 3.   This resolution shall take effect immediately.  Karen J. C arnevale, RMC, Municipal Clerk   Bill Rutherford, Council President  Adopted: September   24 , 2024

9/ 20 /24  1  4885 - 9636 - 3233.v3  Deleted:   ¶  ¶  FIRST AMENDMENT TO REDEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT AND PURCHASE AND SALE  AGREEMENT  THIS   FIRST AMENDMENT TO REDEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT   AND   PURCHASE   AND  SALE   AGREEMENT   ( “ First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement ” )   is made this   ___   day of  September , 20 2 4   (the “ Effective Date ”) , by and between   THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE ,   NEW  JERSEY,   a   public body corporate and politic of   the State of New Jersey with offices at   66 Main Street,  West Orange , New Jersey 070 52   ( “ Seller ”   or the “ Township ” ) ,   and   MDGA   –   WEST ORANGE  DOWNTOWN REDEVEL O PMENT, LLC ,   a New Jersey   limited liability company   with offices at 3 Centre  Drive, Monroe, New Jersey 08831   (“ Purchaser ”   or “ Redeveloper ” ).   Purchaser and Seller are herein  individually referred to as a “ Party ” and collectively as the “ Parties ” .  RECITALS  WHEREAS, Seller is the owner of certain real property   designated as Lots 15, 16 and 51 in Block  115 on the tax maps of the Township of West Orange, New Jersey, which properties are located north of  Lakeside Avenue, west of   Watchung Aven u e and south Columbia Street   ( the   “ Property ” );  WHEREAS,   the Property is   part of   an area designated   by   Seller   as an “area in need of  redevelopment”   pursuant to the provisions of the   Local Redevelopment and Housing Law, N.J.S.A.  40A:12A - 1   et seq ., as amended and supplemented (the “ LRHL ”), and Seller is entering into this   First  Amendment and   Purchase   A greement to convey the Property to Purchaser   as   the designated “redeveloper”  of the Property,   pursuant to   the provisions of the LRHL;  WHEREAS, the Property is subject to the Film Services Overlay District (“ FSOD ”) as set forth in  that certain Fourth Amend ment to the Downtown Redevelopment Plan adopted on   September 14, 2021   (the  “ Redevelopment Plan ”);  WHEREAS , Purchaser desires to construct a   film and video production campus containing between  150,000 and 350,000 square feet (the “ Project ”)   on the Property, which is adjacent to the site of the world’s  first film production studio ;  WHEREAS,   in furtherance of the Project the Parties have   entered   into that certain Redevelopment  Agreement dated December 5, 2023 ,   pursuant to which Purchaser   agrees to construct the Project   on the  Property (the “ Original   Redevelopment Agreement ”)   (the Original Redevelopment Agreement and the  First Amendment and Purchase Agreement collectively referred to herein as the “ Agreements ”) ;  WHEREAS, the   Original   Redevelopment   Agreement   requires Seller to sell the Property to  Purchaser   in connection with the development of the Project ; and  WHEREAS,   the Parties seek to set forth their agreement   regarding the purchase and sale of the  P roperty   in a comprehensive document setting f orth their mutual understanding , as well as to amend the  Original   Redevelopment Agreement to ,   among   other things,   extend the term thereof .  NOW, THEREFORE , in consideration of the above recitals and the mutual promises, covenants  and undertakings contained herein, and for   other   good and valuable consideration ,   the receipt and  sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged ,   the Parties agree as follows:  1.   T HE PROPERTY .  1.1   Seller agrees to sell to Purchaser and P urchaser agrees to buy from   Seller, the  Property ,   together with all of the following, if any (all of which shall be deemed to be included in the

2  4885 - 9636 - 3233.v3  defined term   “ Property ” ): all easements, rights of way or use, licenses, permits and rights to same belonging  to or appurtenant to the Property ; any strips or gores of land adjoining the Property; all mineral, oil, and gas  rights and profits, water rights and sub - terrain rights; all sewer and utility rights allocated to the Property  and the improvements thereon; all right, title, and interest of   Seller   as owner of the Property   in and to any  roads, streets and ways, public or private, serving the Property;   and appurtenances to the Property; all right,  title and interest of Seller   as owner of the Property   in and to any land lying in the bed of any street, road,  avenue, lane or right - of - way in front of, adjoining or adjacent to the Property whether currently existing or  to be obtained by Seller hereunder ;   other than any of the right, title or interest set forth herein that Seller,  as a municipal corporation of the State, may have, or otherwise may be required to hold, preserve, possess,  occupy or maintain, as a   matter of law.  1.2   Lot 51, Block 115 (“ Lot 51 ”) is currently occupied by the owner of adjacent Lot 16.01,  Block 115 on a month to month lease for its   towing operation. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary ,  Purchaser shall have the right to elect not to take title to Lot 51, in which event the Purchase Price shall be  reduced by   Fifty   Three Thousand Two Hundred Eighty ( $53,280 ) Dollars ,   which is the   amount equal to the  size of Lot 51   (.06 acre)   multiplied by Eight Hundred Eighty Eight Thousand ($888,000) Dollars per acre.  2.   PURCHASE PRICE   AND   PAYMENT .   Purchaser   agrees to pay ,   and Seller agrees to accept, as  and for the total purchase price for the Property the sum of   Ten   Million   Eight Hundred Thirty Three  Thousand   Six Hundred   ($ 10 , 833 , 6 00 )   Dollars   (the   “ Purchase Price ” ) .   At Closing, Purchaser shall   pay the  Purchase Price   to Seller , less any sum to be credited to   the Purchase Price   in accordance with the terms of  this Agreement .   The Parties acknowledge that   Purchaser has paid to Seller   the Initial Payment   in the amount  of Two Hundred Thousand ($200,000) Dollars   and the Extension Payment   in the amount of Two Hundred  Thousand ($200,000)   Dollars , pursuant to Section 3.2(a) and (b) of the Original Redevelopment Agreement .  The   Initial Payment and Extension Payment are   non - refundable , except as specifically provided   otherwise i n  Section 3.3 of the Original Redevelopment Agreement ,   and   shall not be   credited to the Purchase Price at  Closing.  3.   ADJUSTMENTS AND PRORATIONS . In the event that final, current real estate tax bills are unavailable at  Closing, such taxes shall be prorated on the basis of the taxes   for the most recent year for which tax bills  are available.  1 9 .3   The obligation of this   Article   16   with regard to any adjustments or payments shall  survive Closing for a period of up to   sixty   ( 6 0)   calendar   days so that the Parties may undertake a post -  closing   “ true - up ”   to the   extent that same is necessary   for such items that may have been estimated at closing.  20 .   ASSESSMENTS .   If   as of the   Closing   Date   the Property, or any part thereof, shall be or  shall have been affected by a special governmental assessment or assessments which are or may become  payable in annual   installments for: (i) improvements that have been completed prior to Closing; or (ii) for  which the first installment is then due or has been paid, then any such installment shall be paid and  discharged by Seller prior to or at Closing.   The Parties acknowledge that , notwithstanding any PILOT,  Financial Agreement and tax exemption granted   thereunder ,   the Property   shall   be subject to payment of the  special assessment for the Downtown Special Improvement District, pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   40:56 - 65,   et seq .  2 1.   ASSIGNMENT .   T he rights granted in this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement are  personal to the Purchaser and this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement is non - assignable. Any   such  assignment shall be void, the assignee shall acquire no rights herein and Seller shall not recognize any such  assignment. Notwithstanding the foregoing,   Purchaser   shall have the right to   convert   to an   u rban   r enewal  e ntity,   pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   40A:20 - 1, et seq.,   and   any such conversion will not be deemed an assignment  Deleted:  Deleted:   is and after Closing  Deleted:   continue to

11  4885 - 9636 - 3233.v3  of this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement   so long as   Donald Epstein   and   Joseph Taylor   remain in  Control of Purchaser .  2 2 .   DEFAULTS .  2 2 .1   Seller’s Default.   In the event Seller fails to perform, or otherwise breaches   any  of the terms of   this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement or any of its representations, warranties or  covenants ,   and Seller has not cured such default within   thirty (30)   days of   receipt   of notice of same from  Purchaser , then   Purchaser shall be entitled to either: (i)   terminate this   First Amendment and Purchase  Agreement; (ii) waive the default or breach and proceed to Closing ; or (iii) pursue an action for specific  performance .   Upon termination   neither Party shall have any further rights or obligations hereunder   except  as expressly set forth herein to the contrary.   In the event Purchaser is successful in an action for specific  performance Purchaser shall receive a credit against the Purchase Price in an amount equal to the expense s  incurred by Purchaser in connection with such action, including, but not limited to, reasonable attorneys ’  fees.  2 2 .2   Purchaser’s Default.   In the event Purchaser fails to perform any of its obligations  under this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement   and   Purchaser has not cured said default within t hirty  ( 3 0) days of   receipt   of notice of same from Seller,   then   Seller shall , as its sole and exclusive remedy,   be  entitled to terminate this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement   and   retain   the Initial Deposit and  Additional Deposit as   liquidated damages   and   neither Party shall have any further rights or obligations  hereunder except for those that specifically survive the termination of this   First Amendment and Purchase  Agreement .  2 2 .3   Cross - Default with   Original   Redevelopment Agreement .   This   First   Amendment  and Purchase   Agreement is made in furtherance of completion of the Project as more specifically described  and set forth in the   Original   Redevelopment Agreement and fulfillment of the obligations of the Parties  under the   Original   Redevelopment Agreement   are necessary and material to the sale of the Property  pursuant to this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement.   Accordingly, a default under this   First  Amendment and Purchase   Agreement shall be deemed a default under the   Original   Redevelopmen t  Agreement, and vice - versa, and   the termination of this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement shall be  deemed to automatically terminate the   Original   Redevelopment Agreement, and vice - versa.  2 3 .   CONDEMNATION AND CASUALTY.  2 3 .1   Casualty.   Prior to the Closing, Seller shall bear all risks of loss to the Property  and all liabilit ies arising from the Property.   For the avoidance of doubt, it is agreed all improvements on  the Property shall be demolished by Seller prior to Closing as part of the Remediation.  2 3 .2   Condemnation.   Seller represents and warrants to Purchaser that Seller has not  received any notice of any condemnation proceeding or other action or proceeding, either contemplated or  pending, in the nature of eminent domain ( “ Condemnation Proceedings ” ) in connection with the   Property;  nor does the Seller have knowledge of any Condemnation Proceedings.   If Seller becomes aware of or  receives, at or prior to the Closing, any notice of Condemnation Proceedings it will immediately advise  Purchaser of   the Condemnation Proceedings in writing and provide a copy of any such notice of  Condemnation Proceedings to Purchaser. If all   of the Property is condemned prior to Closing, this   First  Amendment and Purchase   Agreement shall terminate. If only a   portion of the Property has been or is taken  by condemnation or eminent domai n   prior to Closing,   Purchaser shall have the right to either (i) terminate  this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement, or (i) proceed to Closing and   the Purchase Price shall be  reduced by the total of any award of damages actually received by Seller. If a Condemnation Proceeding  has commenced or notice thereof has been received prior to the Closing,   but same is no t resolved by the  Closing Date, and Purchaser elects to proceed to Closing,   Seller shall assign to Purchaser all of Seller’s

12  4885 - 9636 - 3233.v3  rights, title and interest in and to any awards or damages to which Seller may have become entitled or may  thereafter be entitled by reason of any exercise of the power of eminent domain or condemnation with  respect to or for the taking of the Property or   any portion thereof.   Notwithstanding   anything to the contrary,  in the event   the taking or contemplated taking will have a material and   adverse effect   on Purchaser’s ability  to   construct   and operate the Project, Purchaser shall have the right to terminate   this   First Amendment and  Purchase   Agreement, in which event neither Party shall have any   further   rights or   obligations   hereunder  except for those that specifically survive the termination of this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement.  In any event ,   Seller shall not settle any condemnation proceeding without the consent of Purchaser, in its  sole and absolute discretion.  2 4 .   BROKER CLAUSE .   Each of the   P arties warrants and   represents   to the other   that   it has  not dealt with any real estate agent, broker or   salesperson . E ach of the   P arties   covenants to   indemnify the  other against claims of any such third party.   The indemnity contained in this Section 2 4   shall survive the  C losing.  2 5 .   NOTICES .   Any demand, notice or other communication required or permitted to be given  hereunder shall be in writing, and shall be   (i)   delivered personally,   (ii)   by   nationally recognized   overnight  courier service (such as FedEx   or UPS ) for next   B usiness   D ay   (as defined below)   delivery ,   or by   electronic  mail   to the Parties   at the addresses set forth below (or to such other addresses as the   P arties may specify by  due notice to the other):  If to   Seller :  Township of West Orange  66 Main Street  West Orange,   New Jersey 070 52  Attn:   James P. Abbot t ,   Business   Administrator  Email:   Jabbot t @westorange.org  with copy to:  Robert Beckelman, Esq.  Wilentz, Goldman & Spitzer  90 Woodbridge Center Drive, Suite 900, Box 10  Woodbridge , New Jersey 07 905  Email: Rbeckelman@Wilentz.com  If to   Purchaser :  MDGA   –   West Orange Downtown Redevelopment,   LLC  C/o Matrix Development Group  3 Centre Street  Monroe, New Jersey 08831  Attn: Donald   Epstein   & Joseph Taylor  Email:   Depstein@Matrixcompanies.com   &   Jtaylor@Matrixcompanies.com  with copy to:  Chiesa, Shahinian & Giantomasi PC  105 Eisenhower Parkway  Roseland , New Jersey 070 68  Attn: Stephen A. Kisker

13  4885 - 9636 - 3233.v3  Email: Skisker@CSGLaw.com  Any notice delivered to a Party's designated address by (a) personal delivery shall be deemed  received on the date actually delivered or refused, (b)   nationally   recognized overnight courier service for  next   B usiness   D ay delivery shall be deemed received the next   B usiness   D ay after its deposit with such  nationally recognized overnight courier service   for next Business Day delivery , or (c) by electronic mail  shall be deemed received on the day such electronic mail was sent if sent before 5pm New York time   on   a  Business Day , and shall be deemed received on the next   B usiness   D ay if sent   on a non - Business   Day or  after 5pm New York time   on a Busin e ss Day .   N o electronic mail shall be deemed received if the sending  Party receives a message that same has been delayed, rejected or otherwise not delivered .   Any notice given  by an attorney for a Party shall be effective for all purposes.   A “ Business Day ” is a day that is not a  Saturday, Sunday or Federal holiday.  2 6 .   FURTHER ASSURANCES .   Each of the Parties agrees to execute, acknowledge and  deliver such other documents or instruments as the other may reasonably require from time to time to carry  out the purposes of this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement.  2 7 .   MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS .   The Parties further agree as follows:  2 7 .1   Entire Agreement .   This   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement   and the  Original   Redevelopment Agreement, as amended herein,   contains the entire agreement between the Parties  regarding the   purchase and sale of the   Property and supersedes all prior agreements, and may not be  modified or changed except by an agreement in writing executed by   both   Parties.   Notwithstanding, this  First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement   is made in furtherance of the completion of the Project as more  specifically described and set forth in the   Original   Redevelopment Agreement and fulfillment of the  obligations of the Parties under the   Original   Redevelopm ent Agreement   are necessary and material to the  sale of the Property pursuant to this   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement. Any capitalized terms not  defined herein shall be as defined in the   Original   Redevelopment Agreement and reference shall be made  to the   Original   Redevelopment Agreement   in the event of any ambiguities under this Agreement.   Except  as specifically amended   or stated otherwise   herein, the   Original   Redevelopment Agreement shall remain in  full force and effect.  2 7 .2   Interpretation .   This   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement has been prepared  or reviewed, or both, by counsel for Seller and Purchaser . T he Parties agree th is   Agreement shall be  construed reasonably to carry out its intent without presumption against or in favor of either Party.  2 7 .3   Counterparts   / Execution .   This   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement may  be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed to be an original, but all of which  shall constitute one and the same   Agreement.   Electronic   signatures   to this   First Amendment and Purchase  Agreement   shall be binding for all purposes.   Delivery of an executed counterpart of a signature page of  this document by facsimile or other generally accepted electronic means ( i.e.   e -   mail) shall be effective as  delivery of a manually executed counterpart of this   document .  2 7 .4   Governing Law .   This   First Amendment and Purchase   Agreement shall be  governed by the laws of the State of New Jersey.  2 7 .5   Prevailing Party Provision .   In the event any action or proceeding is commenced  by a Party to obtain a declaration of rights hereunder, to enforce any provision of this   First Amendment and  Purchase   Agreement, or to seek rescission of this Agreement for default under this Agreement, whether  legal or equitable,   the prevailing Party shall be reimbursed by the other for the cost of suit, including  reasonable   legal fees.

14  4885 - 9636 - 3233.v3  2 7 .6   Invalid Terms .   If any term or provision of this   First Amendment and Purchase  Agreement or the application thereof shall, to any extent, be invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of this  Agreement shall not be affected thereby, and each of the remaining terms shall remain as valid and  enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by   law.  2 7 .7   Counting Days .   Whenever used herein, unless expressly provided otherwise, the  term   “ days ”   shall mean consecutive calendar days, except that if the expiration of any time period measured  in days occurs on a Saturday, Sunday   or Federal Reserve holiday , such expiration shall automatically be  extended to the next   Business   D ay.  2 7 .8   Recitals Incorporated .   The Recitals of this   First Amendment and Purchase  Agreement are incorporated into and made a part of the terms of this Agreement.  2 7 . 9   Headings.   All paragraph headings or captions contained in this   First Amendment  and Purchase   Agreement shall be for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the construction or  interpretation of any provision of this Agreement.  2 7 .1 0   Party   Cooperation .   T he Parties shall reasonably cooperate with one another in  furtherance of their efforts to consummate the transaction contemplated by this   First Amendment and  Purchase   Agreement .  IN WITNESS WHEREOF , the Parties hereto have duly executed this   First Amendment and  Purchase   Agreement as of the   Effective Date . pages.text: 283-24  September 24, 2024  RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING THE AWARD OF   A NON-FAIR AND OPEN  CONTRACT   FOR   SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS AT 60-62 VALLEY WAY  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   has   a   need   to   install   safety   improvements   at   62-62  Valley   Way   in   conjunction   with   the   Street   Improvements   2021,   Phase   2   project   that   includes   Valley   Way  from Eagle Rock Avenue to Moore Terrace ;  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   has   met   the   provisions   of   the   NJ   Local   Unit   Pay-to-  Play   Law   to   award   a   contract   under   the   N on- F air   and   O pen   C ontract   pursuant   to   the   provisions   of  N.J.S.A. 19:44A-20.5 ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Municipal   Engineer   by   letter   dated   September   1 8 ,   2024   has   determined   that   the  value of the   contract   will exceed $17,500; and  WHEREAS,   the anticipated term or this contract is one year or less; and  WHEREAS,   Reggio   Construction   Inc.   has   submitted   a   proposal   dated   September   17 ,   2024  indicating   they   will   provide   the   installation   of   bollards   including   excavation,   granite   curb,   traffic   marking  and associated regulatory and warning signs   in the amount   not to exceed $42,240 .0 0 ; and  WHEREAS,   Reggio   Construction ,   Inc.   has   completed   and   submitted   a   Political   Contri bution  Disclosure   Form   which   certifies   that   R eggio   Construction ,   Inc.   has   not   made   any   reportable   contributions  to   a   candidate   committee   in   the   Township   of   West   Orange   in   the   previous   one   year,   and   that   the   contract  will   prohibit   Reggio   Construction   Inc.   from   making   any   reportable   contributions   through   the   term   of   the  contract ;   and  WHEREAS,   sufficient   funds   exist   for   the   services   outlined   in   the   proposal   of   R eggio  Construction   Inc . ; and  NOW   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   that   the   Township   Council   of   the   Town ship   of   West  Orange   authorizes   the   Township   of   West   Orange   to   enter   into   a   contract   with   R eggio   Construction   Inc.   as  described herein; and,  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   Political   Contribution   Disclosure   be   placed   on   file   with  this resolution ; and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   be   published   in   the   West   Orange  Chronicle within ten (10) days of its approval as required by State Statute.  Karen   J.   Carnevale,   R.M.C.,   Municipal Clerk   Bill Rutherford , Council President  Adopted: September 24, 2024  I hereby certify funds are available from:   Account No.:  202 3   Capit a l Account  0 3-2830-23-0500-010  $ 42,240.00  J ohn Ditinyak,   C hief   Financial Officer   Escrow Account  12-6730-00-0100  $38,717.60

#14671212.1  284-24  September   24 , 2024  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS ,   there   was   litigation   initiated   by   Prism   Green   Urban   Renewal   LLC  ( “ Prism ” )   concerning   the   Township ’ s   termination   of   its   redevelopment   rights   under   t he   existing  Redevelopment   Plan,   in   the   Superior   Court   of   New   Jersey,   Law   Division:   Essex   County  captioned   Prism   Green   Associates   IV,   LLC   v.   Township   of   West   Orange,   Robert   D.   Parisi,   John  O.   Gross,   Whoopi   Inc.,   MBS   Group   Holdings,   LLC,   Jeremy   Harlton,   John   W.   Henry ,   Docket  No. ESX-L-5706-21   (the   “ Litigation ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   in   September   2021,   the   Township   and   Prism   settled   the   Litigation   (the  “ Settlement ” ),   by   which,   among   other   things,   the   parties   would   enter   an   agreement   for   Prism   to  sell and convey all of its property in the Township (the   “ Prism Properties ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   on   September   15,   2021,   the   Township   Council   adopted   Ordinance   No.  2651-21   authorizing   the   issuance   of   a   bond   to   satisfy   the   monetary   obligations   of   the  S ettlement; and  WHEREAS,   on   February   28,   2022 ,   the   Township   and   Prism   executed   a   Settlement  Agreement   and   Purchase   and   Sale   Agreement   ( “ PSA ” )   for   the   acquisition   of   the   Prism  P roperti es and resolution of all issues in the Litigation; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   acquired   the   Prism   Properties   pursuant   to   the   Settlement  and the PSA; and  WHEREAS ,   the   PSA   provided   for   an   escrow   to   be   held   from   the   purchase   price  proceeds   in   the   amount   of   $951,0 45   as   an   estimate   for   environmental   remediation   costs   (the  “ Environmental Escrow ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   PSA   provided   for   a   dispute   resolution   mechanism   to   address  disagreements   as   to   the   parties ’   respective   remediation   cost   estimates,   by   referring   final  determination   of   reasonable   remediation   costs   to   an   independent   licensed   site   remediation  professional (LSRP) (the   “ Independent LSRP Determination ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   Independent   LSRP   Determination   resulted   in   an   estate   of   $617,035,  subject   to   two   open   issues   that   could   not   be   fully-determined   by   the   Independent   LSRP  Determination ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   parties   brought   the   matter   of   the   remaining   issues   to   the   Court   for  resolution   or   further   proceedings   to   finally   resolve   the   disp osi t ion   of   the   Environmental   Escrow ,  pursuant to the PSA;   and

#14671212.1  WHEREAS,   the   Township   and   Prism   have   negotiated   a   settlement   of   all   remaining  issues   concerning   the   disposition   of   the   Environmental   Escrow ,   providing   for   the   final   amount   of  the   amount   to   be   maintained   by   the   Township   at   $650,000,   with   no   further   review   and   approvals  of   proposed   disbursements   required   by   Prism,   as   currently   provided   for   under   the   PSA,   with   the  Township   free   to   utilize   the   remaining   proceeds   in   the   Environmental   Escrow   in   connection   with  the   remediation   of   the   Prism   Properties,   in   exchange   for   releasing   the   balance   of   $ 301,045   from  the Environmental Escrow to Prism; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   believes   that   it   is   in   the   best   interest   to   finally   settle   and  resolve   all   outstanding   issues   with   Prism   concerning   the   Environmental   Escrow   to   avoid   the  continued costs and efforts associated with litigating the matter further; and  WHEREAS ,   final   settlement   is   memorialized   in   the   Amendment   to   Purchase   and   Sale  Agreement and Memorandum of Settlement,   in   the   form s   attached hereto.  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the  Amendment   to   Purchase   and   Sale   Agreement   and   Memorandum   of   Settlement,   attached   hereto,  and   the   Municipal   Clerk   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   witness   the   Mayor ’ s   signature   and   the  Mayor   is   hereby   authorized   to   take   all   further   and   necessary   steps   to   effectuate   the   above  a greement s approved herein .  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C. , Municipal Clerk   Bill   Rutherford ,   Council President  Adopted:   September   24 ,   2024  2

1  284-24  AMENDMENT   TO   PURCHASE AND SALE AGREEMENT  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  This   A mendment   made   this   24 th   day   of   September ,   202 4   (the   “ Effective   Date ” )   by   and  between   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ”   or   “ Buyer ” ),   a   body   corporate   and  politic   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   having   its   address   at   66   Main   Street,   West   Orange,   New  Jersey   07052 ;   and   Prism   Green   Associates   IV,   LLC;   Prism   Green   Urban   Renewal,   LLC;   GP   55  Lakeside,   LLC;   GP   Ashland   Urban   Renewal,   LLC;   GP   177   Main,   LLC   (collectively   “ Prism ”   or  “ Seller ” )   having   its   address   at   310   Kingsland   Street,   Nutley,   NJ   07110 ,   and   individually   referred  to as a   “ Party ”   and collectively the   “ Parties. ”  1.   BACKGROUND  1.1   The   Parties   executed   a   Purchase   and   Sale   Agreement   dated   February   28 ,   2022  (the   “ PSA ” )   regarding   certain   real   property   described   therein   (the   “ Property ” )   resulting   in   a  transfer of the Property   by deed delivered   on   February 28, 2022   (the   “ Closing ” ) .  1.2   Since   the   Closing,   certain   remediation   activities   at   the   Property   as   contemplated  in Article XIII of the PSA   have commenced .  1.3   The   Parties   have   reached   agreement   that   given   the   current   status   of   remediation,  the continued remediation of the Property would   completed   a s   provided for herein.  2.   Article 13 is therefore revised and replaced as follows:  X I II,   ENVIRONMENTAL PROVISIONS  13.1   Presence of Contamination .  (a)   As   of   the   Closing,   Seller   represent ed   the   following   to   the   best   of   Seller's  knowledge   and   belief,   that   other   than   as   set   forth   in   the   Environmental   Documents :   (i)   the  Property   is   in   compliance   with   all   applicable   local,   federal   and   state   statutes   or   regulations,  ordinances,   permits,   orders,   and   directives   regarding   pollutants,   contaminants,   hazardous  waste,   materials   and/or   substances   (hereinafter   collectively   referred   to   as   "contamination")  and/or   solid   waste;   (ii)   there   is   no   pending   or   contemplat ed   proceeding   or   administrative   action  arising   out   of   the   environmental   condition   of   the   Property;   and   (iii)   the   Property   has   never   been  remediated.  (b)   Seller(s)   further   represent ed   and   represents   (s)   that:   (i)   it   has   not  discharged   any   hazardous   substances   or   waste   on   the   Property;   (ii)   it   has   no   knowledge   of   any  discharge   of   hazardous   substances   or   waste   on   the   Property   other   than   as   set   forth   in   the  Environmental   Documents;   (iii)   it   did   not   deposit   or   cause   to   be   deposited   solid   waste   on   the  Property;   (iv)   it   has   no   knowledge   of   the   presence   of   solid   waste   on   or   buried   in   the   Property;  and   (v)   the   Property   does   not   require   remediation   except   as   noted   in   the   Environmental

2  Documents.   The   foregoing   notwithstanding,   the   Parties   acknowledge   the   existence   of   the  Stockpiled Soil as defined in Section 13.5(f).  (c)   Buyer   acknowledges   that :   (i)   the   Property   has   known   environmental  contamination   and   hazardous   substances   requiring   remediation   which   exist   at,   on,   under   and/or  from   the   Property;   (ii)   the   Property   is   undergoing   remediation   work   in   accordance   with   the  requirements   adopted   by   the   New   Jersey   Department   of   Environmental   Protection   ( “ NJDEP ” ),  including   requ irements   and   timeframes   contained   in   the   ISRA ,   as   defined   in   Section   13.5   below,  and   the   Spill   Compensation   and   Control   Act,   N.J.S.A.   58:10- 23.11   ( “ the   Spill   Act ” );   and   (iii)  additional   remediation at the Property will be needed.  13.2   Buyer   acknowledges   that   the   Purchase   Price   together   with   the  Environmental   Escrow   provided   for   in   Section   13.9   reflect ed   the   Property ’ s   present   known  environmental   condition.   The   Purchase   Price   together   with   the   Environmental   Escrow   include d   a  deduction,   offset,   and   credit   for   any   remediation   costs.   The   Purchase   Price   did   not   reflect   any  deduction, offset, and credit for the Stockpiled Soils as defined hereinafter.  13.3   The   Parties ’   intent   is   that,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   58:10 -23.11g.d(4),   the  Township   is   not   liable   for   the   cleanup   and   removal   costs   of   any   discharge   which   occurred   or  began   prior   to   ownership   by   the   Township,   or   for   any   fees,   penalties   and/or   enforcement   actions  relating   to   alleged   non-compliance   with   ISRA ,   as   defined   in   Section   13.5   below,   or   other  applicable law prior to the Closing.  13.4   As   between   the   Parties,   and   subject   to   the   Environmental   Escrow   and   the  use   of   same   as   provided   for   herein,   the   Township   shall   be   responsible,   as   required   by   applicable  law, for the actual costs of all remediation of the Property.  13.5   ISRA Compliance.  (a)   The   Parties   acknowledge   that   the   Industrial   Site   Recovery   Act,   N.J.S.A.  13:1K-6   et   seq.   ( “ ISRA ” )   had   previously   been   triggered   with   regard   to   the   Property   and   that  there   are   three   (3)   open   ISRA   cases,   Case   Nos.   20020198,   200700415,   and   20070213,   and   that  ISRA   was   triggered   by   the   sale   of   the   Property   to   Buyer .   NJDEP   has   assigned   Program   Interest  No. 027166 for the remediation of the Property (collectively, the   “ ISRA Cases ” ).  (b)   Prior   to   the   Closing   a   General   Information   Notice   ( “ GIN ” )   was   filed   with  the NJDEP and Seller ’ s LSRP issued a Remediation Certification.  (c)   The   Township   negotiated   with   the   NJDEP   to   receive   an   adjustment   of  certain   remedial   timeframes   applicable   to   Seller,   which   extension   was   previously   denied   and  would   otherwise   be   unavailable   to   Seller,   on   the   condition   that   the   Township ’ s   LSRP   be   permitted  to   remediate   the   Property   upon   closing,   notwithstanding   the   Remediation   Certification   language.  Prior   to   Closing,   the   Township   submit ted   to   the   NJDEP:   1)   an   Exemption   from   Spill   Act   Liability  Certification   Form,   and   2)   a   Remedial   Timeframe   Notification   For m   with   an   Extension   Request  ( “ Extension   Request ” )   to   extend   the   Remedial   Investigation   Report   timeframe,   along   with   a   cover

3  letter   memorializing   the   Township ’ s   discussions   with   the   NJDEP   and   NJDEP   granted   the  Extension Request.  (d)   The   remediation   shall   be   conducted   through   the   issuance   of   a   site-wide  Response   Action   Outcome   (as   that   term   is   defined   at   N.J.S.A.   58:10B-1   et   seq.)   to   close   the  ISRA Cases.  13.6   Remediation After Closing.  (a)   The   Township   will   cause   the   remediation   of   the   Property   to   be  completed   as   provided   for   in   Paragraph   13.5(d)   and   shall   be   the   “ Person   Responsible   for  Conducting the Remediation ”   as that term is defined at .   N.J.S.A. 58:10B-1 , et seq .  (b)   P rovided   that   the   remediation   is   completed   as   provided   for   in  Paragraph   13.5(d),   there   are   no   limitation s   or   restriction s   on   the   manner   by   which   the   Property  is remediated.  (c)   The   Township   will   cause   any   needed   Remediation   Funding   Source  or any needed Financial Assurance to be funded .  (d)   Releases and   Indemnifications.  (i)   “ Seller   Related   Parties ”   shall   mean   Seller   and   any   agent,   advisor,  representative,   affiliate,   employee,   director,   officer,   partner,   member,   beneficiary,   investor,  servant,   shareholder,   trustee   or   other   person   or   entity   (each,   a   “ Person ” )   acting   on   Seller ’ s  behalf or otherwise related to or affiliated with Seller.  (ii)   “ Township   Related   Parties ”   shall   mean   the   Township   Related  Parties   and   any   agent,   advisor,   representative,   affiliate,   employee,   director,   officer,   partner,  member,   beneficiary,   investor,   servant,   shareholder,   trustee   or   other   person   or   entity   (each,   a  “ Person ” )   acting   on   the   Township ’ s   behalf   or   otherwise   related   to   or   affiliated   with   the  Township   (including   subsequent   purchasers   of   the   Property   with   whom   the   Township   executes   a  redevelopment agreement for the Property) .  (iii)   “ Claims ”   shall   mean   all   claims,   demands,   causes   of   action,   losses,  damages,   liabilities,   costs   and   expenses   (including,   without   limitation,   reasonable   attorneys ’   fees  and   disbursements).  (iv)   Seller   shall   indemnify   and   hold   the   Township   Related   Parties  harmless ;   Seller   on   its   behalf   and   on   behalf   of   the   Seller   Related   Parties   forever   RELEASE,  REMISE,   WAIVE   AND   FOREVER   DISCHARGE   the   Township   Related   Parties   from   Claims   to  the   extent   relating   to:   (A)   Seller ’ s   remediation   of   the   Property   prior   to   Closing;   and   (B)  enforcement   actions,   fines,   assessments,   or   penalties   relating   to   actions,   inactions   or   alleged  non-compliance   prior   to   the   Closing,   except   to   the   extent   such   claims   are   the   result   of   new  discharges   on   the   Property   after   the   Closing   or   actions   or   inactions   by   the   Township,   the  Township ’ s contractors, agents, designees, assignees or successor in title.

4  (v)   The   Township   shall   indemnify   and   hold   the   Seller   Related   Parties  harmless ;   the   Township   on   its   behalf   and   on   behalf   of   the   Township   Related   Parties   RELEASE,  REMISE,   WAIVE   AND   FOREVER   DISCHARGE   the   Seller   Related   Parties   from   Claims   relating  to:   (A)   the   manner   by   which   the   remediation   of   the   Property   is   conducted   after   Closing;   and   (B)  fines,   assessments,   or   penalties   relating   to   actions   after   the   Closing,   except   to   the   extent   such  claims   are   the   result   of   discharges   on   the   Property   prior   to   the   Closing   or   a ctions   or   inactions  by   the   Seller   Related   Parties,   including   the   exacerbation   of   existing   contamination   during   the  Township ’ s remediation of the Property .  (vi)   (e)   Seller   and   the   Township   shall   execute   the   Memoranda   of  Settlement appended hereto as Exhibit 13.6 to be recorded.  (f)   Stockpiled   Soils .   Upon   information   and   belief,   Seller   contend ed   that  certain   soils,   identified   on   Exhibit   11   attached   hereto,   are   currently   staged   on   the   Property   and  were   excavated   by   the   Seller   from   public   rights-of-way   in   connection   with   the   installation   of  certain   utility   lines,   but   the   Parties   have   not   confirmed   that   this   excavation   accounts   for   the   full  volume   of   soil   or   other   materials   staged   on   the   Property   (the   “ Stockpiled   Soils ” ).   At   Closing,   a  Soil   Characterization   Workplan   escrow   account   w as   funded   from   the   sale   proceeds   in   the   amount  of   Five   Hundred   Thousand   ($500,000.00)   Dollars   and   established   with   the   law   firm   of   Sills  Cummis   &   Gross,   PC.,   in   its   Attorney   Trust   Account   to   be   held   in   accordance   with   this   Agreement  (the   “ Hazardous   Soils   Disposal   Escrow ” ) .   The   resolution   of   the   Stockpiled   Soils   was   achieved ,  and   the   remainder   of   the   Hazardous   Soils   Disposal   Escrow   was   released   to   Sellers.   The   Seller  shall   have   no   further   responsibility   for   the   Stockpiled   Soils   which   the   Township   will   cause   to   be  addressed at no cost to Sellers.  13.7   Deleted.  13.8   PRCR Transfer .  The   Township   shall   within   sixty   ( 6 0)   days   of   this   Amendment   identify   the   Party   Responsible   for  Conducting   the Remediation (the   “ PRCR Transferee ” ).  13.9   Environmental Escrow Account.  (a)   At   the   Closing,   an   environmental   escrow   account   was   funded   by   Prism  and   held   with   Wilentz   Goldman   &   Spitzer   in   the   amount   of   $951,045   (the   “ Environmental  Escrow ” ).  (b)   Within   fifteen   (15)   days   of   the   execution   of   this   Amendment,   the   amount   of  $301,045   and   any   accrued   interest   shall   be   released   to   Seller   leaving   $650,000   remaining   in   the  Environmental   Escrow   (the   “ Remediation   Escrow   Release ” )   and   the   remainder   of   the  Environmental   Escrow   will   be   within   the   control   of   the   Township   to   be   used   in   the   sole  discretion of the Township .

5  (c)   Following   the   Remediation   Escrow   Release,   Seller   shall   have   no   claims   to  the Environmental Escrow.  (d)   Deleted.  (e)   Deleted.  13.10   Seller   and   the   Seller   Related   Parties ,   shall   reasonably   cooperate   with   the  Township   and   its   professionals   as   to   any   environmental   issues   and   do   hereby   specifically   authorize  and   direct   all   of   the   Seller ’ s   professionals,   employees   or   agents,   including   but   not   limited   to,   the  Seller ’ s   environmental   consultants,   and   any   LSRPs   having   had   authority   over   the   Property,   to  cooperate   with   the   Township   as   to   any   and   all   environmental   issues.   Seller ’ s   cooperation   shall  also   include,   but   not   be   limited   to,   providing   the   Township   with   copies   of   all   corresp ondence,  reports   and   documents   that   reflect   the   environmental   condition   of   the   Property,   including   but   not  limited   to   documents   reflecting   any   environmental   investigations   and/or   remediation   of   the  Property,   and   shall   supplement   the   Environmental   Due   Diligence   Materials   with   any   such  documents created after execution of this Agreement before and after Closing.  13.11   This   Agreement   will   not   in   any   way   affect   the   jurisdiction   of   the   NJDEP  over   the   Property   or   the   rights   and   obligations   of   any   third   parties   over   whom   the   Parties   do   not  exercise control .  13.12   This Article XIII shall survive Closing.  3.   All other provisions of the PSA not altered or modified herein   shall   remain as written.  4.   Counterparts.   This   Amendment   may   be   simultaneously   executed   in   several   counterparts,  each   of   which   shall   be   an   original   and   all   of   which   shall   constitute   but   one   and   the   same  instrument.  [SIGNATURE PAGES FOLLOW]

pages.text: 286- 24  September 24 , 2024  RESOLUTION TO HOLD A CLOSED SESSION  WHEREAS , the Open Public Meetings Act (N.J.S.A. 10:4. et seq.) provides for the  exclusion of the public meetings of the governing body during the discussion of certain matters;  and  WHEREAS , prior to the exclusion of the public from a meeting of the Township  Council it is required that the Council adopt a Resolution stating the general nature of the subject  to be discussed and stating as precisely as possible the time when the minutes of the discussi on  conducted in closed session   can be disclosed to the public; and  WHERE AS , this body is about to consider a matter which falls within the purview of  N.J.S.A. 10-4-12, and can properly exclude the public from such discussions; now, therefore, be  it  BE IT   RESOLVED , that the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, now  assembled in public session   at   its regular meeting of   September 24, 2024   enter into a closed  session to consider the following matter(s).  ( )   1.   Discussion of any material, the disclosure of which constitutes an   i nvasion of  individual privacy .  (   )   2 .   A collective bargaining agreement or the terms of provisions of same.  ( )   3.   Techniques or tactic utilized to protect the safety and property of the public  provided that their disclosure could impair such protection.  ( )   4.   An investigation   of violations o r   possible violations of the laws of th e State of  New Jersey   and/ or   the Township of West Orange Administrative Code.  ( X )   5.   Pending or anticipated litigation or contract negotiation in which this body is   or  maybe a party.  ( )   6.   A matter falling within the attorney-client privilege to the extent that  confidentiality is   r equired in order for the attorney to   exercise his ethical duties to this body.  ()   7.   Personnel matters.  (   )   8.   Discussion of any matter which by express provision of Federal law or State  sta tute or rule of Court shall be rendered confidential.  ( )   9.   Discussion   of any matter in which the release of information would impair   a right  to receive funds from the Government of the United Sates.  ()   10 .   Discussion of any matter involving the purchase, lease or acquisition   of   real  property with public funds, the setting   of banking   rates or investment of public funds, where it  could adversely affect the public interest if discussion of such matters were disclosed.

( )   11.   Deliberations   occurring after a public hearing that may result in imposition of a  civil penalty or suspension or loss of a license or permit.  More specifically, the Council   may   discuss in closed session the following topic ( s ) :  282-24   Resolution   o f   t he Township   o f West Orange Authorizing   t he Execution   o f   a   Purchase  Agreement   a nd Amendment   t o Redevelopment Agreement With M DGA –   West Orange  Downtown Redevelopment, L LC (Legal-Beckelman)  284-24   Resolution Authorizing Environmental Escrow Agreement   between the Township of  West Orange and Prism Green Associates IV, LLC (Legal-Beckelman)  28 5 -24   Resolution Authorizing Settlement of the Township of West Orange's Claims - Township  of West Orange v. Ron Jolyn Realty et. al. (Legal-Moon)  (the general subject matter ( s )   of discussion shall be indicated by a check mark in the appropriate box  and shall be more specifically referred to thereafter to the extent suc h   additional disclosure can be  made without compromising the need for confidentiality giving rise to the closed session.  IT IS FURTH E R   RESOLVED   that   the minutes of said discussion shall be made public as  soon as the matter under discussion is no longer of a confidential or sensitive nature such that the  public interest will no longer   be served by such confidentiality.  The foregoing   r esolution was duly adopted by the Township Council of the Township of West  Orange at a public meeting held   on   September 24, 2024 .  Motion :  Second :  Vote  Aye :  Opposed :  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford  M unicipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   September 24, 2024
