#14581359.1 168182.010  TOWNSHIP OF   WEST ORANGE  COUNTY OF ESSEX, STATE OF NEW JERSEY  ORDINANCE NUMBER   28 72 -24  BOND   ORDINANCE   PROVIDING   FOR   ROAD ,   SIDEWALK  AND   SEWER   IMPROVEMENTS ,   BY   AND   IN   THE  T OWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF  ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY;   APPROPRIATING  $ 2,000 ,000   THEREFOR   AND   AUTHORIZING   THE  ISSUANCE   OF   $ 1,904 ,000   BONDS   OR   NOTES   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   TO FINANCE PART OF THE COSTS THEREOF  BE   IT   ORDAINED   AND   ENACTED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF  THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW  JERSE Y   (not   less   than   two-thirds   of   all   the   members   thereof   affirmatively   concurring),   AS  FOLLOWS:  SECTION   1 .   The   improvements   or   purposes   described   in   Section   3   of   this  bond   ordinance   are   hereby   authorized   as   general   improvements   or   purposes   to   be  undertaken   by   the   Township   of   West   Orange ,   in   the   County   of   Essex ,   State   of   New   Jersey  (the   “ Township ” ).   For   the   said   improvement s   or   purpose s   stated   in   Section   3,   there   is  hereby   appropriated   the   amount   of   $ 2,000,000 ,   said   amount   being   inclusive   of   $ 9 6,00 0   as  the   amount   of   down   payment   for   said   improvements   or   purposes   as   required   by   the   Local  Bond   Law,   N.J.S.A.   § 40 A:2-1   et   seq .   (the   “ Local   Bond   Law ” ).   The   down   payment   is   now  available   by   virtue   of   a   provision   or   provisions   in   a   previously   adopted   budget   or   budgets  of the   Township   for down payment or for capital improvement purposes.  SECTION   2 .   For   the   financing   of   said   improvements   or   purposes   described  in   Section   3   hereof   and   to   meet   the   part   of   said   $ 2,000 ,000   appropriation   not   provided   for  by   application   hereunder   of   said   down   payment ,   negotiable   bonds   of   the   Township   are  hereby   authorized   to   be   issued   in   the   principal   amount   not   exceeding   $ 1,904 , 0 0 0   pursuant

2  #14581359.1 168182.010  to ,   and   within   the   limitations   prescribed   by ,   the   Local   Bond   Law.   In   anticipation   of   the  issuance   of   said   bonds   and   to   temporarily   finance   said   improvements   or   purposes,  negotiable   no tes   of   the   Township   in   the   principal   amount   not   exceeding   $ 1,904 ,000   are  hereby   authorized   to   be   issued   pursuant   to ,   and   within   the   limitations   prescribed   by ,   said  Local Bond Law.  SECTION   3 .   (a)   The   improvements   hereby   authorized   and   purposes   for  the   financing   of   which   said   obligations   are   to   be   issued   are   for   road ,   sidewalk   and   sewer  improvements   and   repairs   throughout   the   Township,   including   but   not   limited   to ,   Valley  Road   (from   Kingsley   Street   to   Northfield   Avenue),   Carlson   Road,   and   Colonial   Woods  Drive .   Such   improvements   or   purposes   include,   but   are   not   limited   to,   as   applicable,  demolition   and   excavation,   milling,   paving,   resurfacing   and   reconstruction   of   the   roadway,  roadway   paintin g   and   striping,   the   repairing   and/or   removal   and   installation   of   sidewalks,  driveway   aprons,   curbing,   retaining   walls   and   curb   ramps,   guardrails,   road   shoulders,  gutters,   storm   drains,   and   concrete   improvements,   and   all   other   related   improvements,  design   work,   preparation   of   plans   and   specifications,   permits,   bid   documents,   contract  administration,   work,   materials,   equipment,   accessories,   labor   and   appurtenances  necessary therefor or incidental thereto.  (b)   The   estimated   maximum   amount   of   bonds   or   notes   to   be   issued   for  said improvements or purposes is   $ 1,904 ,000 .  (c)   The   estimated   cost   of   said   improvements   or   purposes   is   $ 2,000, 000 ,  the   excess   amount   thereof   over   the   said   estimated   maximum   amount   of   bonds   or   notes   to  be   issued   therefor   is   the   amount   of   $ 9 6,0 0 0   appropriated   for   the   down   payment   for   said  improvements or   purposes.

3  #14581359.1 168182.010  SECTION   4 .   In   the   event   the   United   States   of   America,   the   State   of   New  Jersey,   and/or   the   County   of   Essex   make   a   contribution   or   grant   in   aid   to   the   Township   for  the   improvements   and   purposes   authorized   hereby   and   the   same   shall   be   received   by   the  Township   prior   to   the   issuance   of   the   bonds   or   notes   authorized   in   Section   2   hereof,   then  the   amount   of   such   bonds   or   notes   to   be   issued   shall   be   reduced   by   the   amount   so  received   from   the   United   States   of   America,   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   and/or   the   County   of  Essex .   In   the   event,   however,   that   any   amount   so   contributed   or   granted   by   the   United  States   of   America,   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   and/or   the   County   of   Essex   shall   be   received  by   the   Township   after   the   issuance   of   the   bonds   or   notes   authorized   in   Section   2   hereof,  then   such   funds   shall   be   applied   to   the   payment   of   the   bonds   or   notes   so   issued   and   shall  be   used   for   no   other   purpose.   This   Section   4   shall   not   apply,   however,   with   respect   to   any  contribution   or   grant   in   aid   received   by   the   Township   as   a   result   of   using   f unds   from   this  bond ordinance as   “ matching local funds ”   to receive such contribution or grant in aid.  SECTION   5 .   All   bond   anticipation   notes   issued   hereunder   shall   mature   at  such   times   as   may   be   determined   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Township ,   provided  that   no   note   shall   mature   later   than   one   (1)   year   from   its   date.   The   notes   shall   bear  interest   at   such   rate   or   rates   and   be   in   such   form   as   may   be   determined   by   the   Chief  Financial   Officer.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Township   shall   determine   all   matters  in   connection   with   the   notes   issued   pursuant   to   this   bond   ordinance,   and   the   signature   of  the   C hief   Financial   Officer   upon   the   notes   shall   be   conclusive   evidence   as   to   all   such  determinations.   All   notes   issued   hereunder   may   be   renewed   from   time   to   time   in  accordance   with   the   provisions   of   the   Local   Bond   Law.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   is  hereby   authorized   to   sell   part   or   all   of   the   notes   from   time   to   time   at   a   public   or   private   sale

4  #14581359.1 168182.010  and   to   deliver   them   to   the   purchaser   thereof   upon   receipt   of   payment   of   the   purchase   price  and   accrued   interest   thereon   from   their   dates   to   the   date   of   delivery   thereo f.   The   Chief  Financial   Officer   is   directed   to   report   in   writing   to   the   governing   body   at   the   meeting   next  succeeding   the   date   when   any   sale   or   delivery   of   the   notes   pursuant   to   this   bond  ordinance   is   made.   Such   report   must   include   the   principal   amount,   the   description,   the  interest   rate,   the   maturity   schedule   of   the   notes   so   sold,   the   price   obtained   and   the   name  of the purchaser.  SECTION   6 .   The   Township   hereby   certifies   that   it   has   adopted   a   capital  budget   or   temporary   capital   budget,   as   applicable.   The   capital   budget   or   temporary   capital  budget ,   as   applicable,   of   the   Township   is   hereby   amended   to   conform   with   the   provisions  of   this   bond   ordinance,   and   to   the   extent   of   any   inconsistency   herewith,   a   resolution   in   the  form   promulgated   by   the   Local   Finance   Board   showing   full   detail   of   the   amended   capital  budget   and   capital   programs   as   approved   by   the   Director   of   the   Division   of   Local  Governm ent   Services,   New   Jersey   Department   of   Community   Affairs,   will   be   on   file   in   the  office of the Clerk and will be available for public inspection.  SECTION   7 .   The   following   additional   matters   are   hereby   determined,  declared, recited and stated:  ( a )   The   purposes   described   in   Section   3   of   this   bond   ordinance   are   not  current   expenses   and   are   improvements   which   the   Township   may   lawfully   undertake   as  general   improvements,   and   no   part   of   the   cost   thereof   has   been   or   shall   be   specially  assessed on property specially benefited thereby.

5  #14581359.1 168182.010  ( b )   The   average   period   of   usefulness   of   said   improvements   within   the  limitations   of   said   Local   Bond   Law,   according   to   the   reasonable   life   thereof   computed   from  the date of the said bonds authorized by this bond ordinance, is   ten   ( 10 )   years.  ( c )   The   supplemental   debt   statement   required   by   the   Local   Bond   Law  has   been   duly   made   and   filed   in   the   Office   of   the   Clerk   of   the   Township   and   a   complete  executed   duplicate   thereof   has   been   filed   in   the   Office   of   the   Director   of   the   Division   of  Local   Government   Services,   Department   of   Community   Affairs,   State   of   New   Jersey,   and  such   statement   shows   that   the   gross   debt   of   the   Township ,   as   defined   in   the   Local   Bond  Law ,   is   increased   by   the   authorization   of   the   bonds   or   notes   provided   for   in   this   bond  ordinance   by   $ 1,904 ,000   and   the   said   obligations   authorized   by   this   bond   ordinance   will  be within all debt limitations prescribed by said Local Bond Law.  ( d )   An   aggregate   amount   not   exceeding   $ 200 ,000   for   items   of   expense  listed   in   and   permitted   under   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-20   of   the   Local   Bond   Law   is   included   in   the  estimated cost indicated herein for the purposes or improvements herein before described.  SECTION   8 .   The   full   faith   and   credit   of   the   Township   are   hereby   pledged   to  the   punctual   payment   of   the   principal   of   and   the   interest   on   the   bond s   or   notes   authorized  by   this   bond   ordinance.   The   bond s   and   notes   authorized   in   this   bond   ordinance   shall   be  direct,   unlimited   obligations   of   the   Township   and ,   unless   paid   from   other   sources,   the  Township   shall   be   obligated   to   levy   ad   valorem   taxes   upon   all   the   taxable   property   within  the   Township   for   the   payment   of   the   principal   of   the   bonds   or   notes   and   the   interest  ther eon without limitation as to rate or amount.  SECTION   9.   The   Township   reasonably   expects   to   reimburse   any  expenditures   toward   the   costs   of   the   improvements   or   purposes   described   in   Section   3

6  #14581359.1 168182.010  hereof   and   paid   prior   to   the   issuance   of   any   bonds   or   notes   authorized   by   this   bond  ordinance   with   the   proceeds   of   such   bonds   or   notes.   This   Section   9   is   intended   to   be   and  hereby   is   a   declaration   of   the   Township 's   official   intent   to   reimburse   any   expenditures  toward   the   costs   of   the   improvements   or   purposes   describ ed   in   Section   3   hereof   to   be  incurred   and   paid   prior   to   the   issuance   of   bonds   or   notes   authorized   herein   in   accordance  with Treasury Regulations   § 1.150-2.  SECTION   10.   The   Township   covenants   to   maintain   the   exclusion   from  gross   income   under   Section   103(a)   of   the   Internal   Revenue   Code   of   1986,   as   amended,   of  the interest on all   tax-exempt   bonds and notes issued under this bond ordinance.  SECTION   11.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Township   is   hereby  authorized   to   prepare   and   to   update   from   time   to   ti me   as   necessary   a   financial  disclosure   document   to   be   distributed   in   connection   with   the   sale   of   obligations   of   the  Township ,   which   are   authorized   herein,   and   to   execute   such   disclosure   document   on  behalf   of   the   Township .   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   is   further   authorized   to   enter   into  the   appropriate   undertaking   to   provide   secondary   market   disclosure   on   behalf   of   the  Township   pursuant   to   Rule   15c2-12   of   the   Securities   and   Exchange   Commission   (the  "Rule")   for   the   benefit   of   holders   and   beneficial   owners   of   bonds   or   notes   of   the  Township ,   which   are   authorized   herein,   and   to   amend   such   undertaking   from   time   to  time   in   connection   with   any   change   in   law,   or   interpretation   thereof,   provided   such  undertaking   is   and   continues   to   be,   in   the   opinion   of   a   nationally   recognized   bond  counsel,   consistent   with   the   requirements   of   the   Rule.   In   the   event   that   the   Township  fails   to   comply   with   its   undertaking,   the   Township   shall   not   be   liable   for   any   monetary  damages, and the remedy shall be limited to specific performance o f the undertaking.

7  #14581359.1 168182.010  SECTION   12.   This   bond   ordinance   shall   take   effect   twenty   (20)   days   after  the   first   publication   of   this   bond   ordinance   after   final   adoption,   as   provided   by   the   Local  Bond Law. 2873-24  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance will   extend the 8AM-10AM No Parking Zone on Oakridge Road to the southerly Dead  End, and create a new 8AM-10AM No Parking Zone on Colony Drive West to discourage all day parking  by Seton Hall Prep students as they arrive for their school day, and allow parking at other times for the  residents. This change was recommended by the Traffic Division of WOPD.

2874-24  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance will   relocate the School Bus Stops from   Gregory Place   and move them to G ilbert   Place   in  front of Roosevelt Middle School . The Parent/Student automobile drop off zone will be relocated to  Gregory Place ,   where parking will not be allowed, but will be posted as a school drop zone,   and Stopping  and Standing will be prohibited on the opposite side of Gregory Place during school   drop off h ours.  The Municipal Engineer and   O fficers of the West Orange Police Traffic Division me t with the   Roosevelt  Middle School Principal and   the   W.O.   Superintendent of Schools   on   September 10 , 2024 to discuss  student drop off   c oncerns , which includes accommodating special needs students with a bus drop off  area   in front of the school entrance.

Subsection s   7-32.2, Restricted Parking Zones   of the Revised General Ordinances  of the Township of West Orange   is   hereby supplemented and amended as follows ( italic type denotes  deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  7-32.2   Restricted Parking Zones. Pursuant to N.J.S.A 39:4-197.6, Restricted Parking Zones, as  defined in N.J.S.A. 39:4-207.6e, are established in front of the following residences as indicated on the  Tax Maps of the Township of West Orange, at the specified locations. Parking within a specific zone is  restricted to the motor vehicle issued a permit pursuant to N.J.S.A. 39:4-197.7. In accordance with  N.J.S.A., 39:4-197.6 parking is permitted in the Restricted Parking Zone except when prohibited in front  of   the residence by other sections of this Chapter. Section 2.   If any provision of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any other provisions or applications of this  Ordinance that can be given effect without the invalid provision(s) or application(s) and to this end the  provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are  hereby repealed.  Section 4.   This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in accordance  with law and upon the approval of the New Jersey Department of Transportation if required.  Susan McCartney, Mayor   Bill Rutherford, Council President  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk  Introduced:   September 24 , 2024  Adopted:   October 8 , 2024

2875-24  L EGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordi nance is in response to   request s   from   handicappe d residen t s   who   reside   at   5 0 Hazel  Avenue, 29 Park Drive   North , and 55 Harrison   Avenue .  By adopting this Ordinance, the Township will establish a handicapped par king stall adjacent to  each of   these   address es . Such parking stalls are permissible under the laws of New Jersey, and they are  designated as Restricted Parking Zones.   All other   P arking   O rdinances under this chapter   apply and  supersede th e use of these restricted spaces.

2876-24  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance will   delete the present ban on parking on the east side of Oxford Terrace during the  period from November 15 th   and April 15 th . This change was requested by residents and approved by the  Police Chief and Municipal Engineer.   This change aligns the parking regulations on Oxford Terrace with  all the other streets in the neighborhood, which ban overnight parking between 2AM-5AM.

1  2878-24  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING CHAPTER   25   OF THE  REVISED   GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BA NNING   “ POP-UP ”   PARTIES  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   MUNICIPAL   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   NEW   JERSEY   that   Chapter   25   hereby   is   and   amended   and   supplemented   to  add   a   new   section   governing   “ pop-up ”   parties   as   defined   herein   within   the   Township   of   West  Orange :  §   1 — Scope   and Purpose :  a.   The   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   has   received   numerous   complaints   from  residents   in   various   neighborhoods   throughout   the   Township   regarding   parties,   events,  and/or   gatherings   wherein   the   attendees   of   the   events   have   purchased   tickets   or  wristbands   for   entry   into   residential   homes   within   the   Township.   The   purpose   of   this  Section   is   to   provide   recourse   for   the   Township   if   such   “ pop-up ”   parties   (defined   herein)  occur   in   the   future   and   to   put   the   public   on   notice   that   the   same   are   prohibited   as   a   h ealth  and safety concern for the general welfare of the public .  §   2 — Definitions:  “ Advertisement ”   shall   be   defined   for   the   purposes   of   this   Ordinance   to   include   the  creation   of   any   flyer,   social   media   posting,   text   message,   or   other   form   of   electronic  communication soliciting guests to a residential home for payment of any amount.  “ Pop-Up   Party(ies) ”   shall   be   defined   as   a   party   for   profit   including   any   event  and/or   gathering   occurring   in   a   residential   home,   apartment   or   other   location  (excluding   commercial   establishments   such   as   licensed   restaurants,   bars,   or   other  event   halls   where   such   activity   is   permitted )   whereby   the   owner,   occupant   and/or  operator   of   the   residence   solicits   or   advertises   the   use   the ir   residence   for   an   event,  party,   or   gathering   and   where   the   guests   are   subject   to   payment   for   tickets,  wristbands, or other mode of   entry into the residence.  §   3 — Prohibitions:  a.   Any   person,   owner,   occupant,   or   operator   of   a   residence   located   in   the   residential  zoning   district   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   shall   be   prohibited   from   advertising  or   otherwise   soliciting   monies   for   entry   into   their   home   to   attend   any   Pop-Up   Party  as defined herein.  b.   Any   person   who   rents   or   otherwise   loans   their   property   in   the   residential   district   of  the Township of West Orange to a person for a Pop-Up Party is prohibited.  c.   Any   person   who   creates,   submits,   or   otherwise   posts   an   Advertisement   for   a   Pop-  Up   Party   shall   be   considered   a   separate   violation   of   this   Ordinance   subject   to   the  penalties provided for herein.

2  d.   Exemptions:   Activities,   gatherings,   or   other   events   that   may   constitute   a   Pop-Up  Party   under   this   Ordinance   shall   be   exempted   where   the   proceeds   for   the   same   shall  be   used   for   any   charitable   organization   licensed   in   the   State   of   New   Jersey   and/or  for political contributions to any local, state, or federal campaign.  §   4 — Enforcement  a.   The   Township   Police   Department,   the   Township   Health   Department,   the   Township  Zoning   Official,   the   Township   Fire   Department,   their   agents,   employees,   officers,  or assigns shall have the power to enforce the provisions of this Ordinance.  §   5 — Penalties:  a.   All   penalties   for   violation   of   any   provision   of   this   section   shall   be   governed   by   the  provisions   of   Section   1-5   et   seq.   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the  Township of West Orange.  §   6 — Severability:  Each   section,   subsection,   sentence,   clause   and   phrase   of   this   Ordinance   is   declared  to   be   an   independent   section,   subsection,   sentence,   clause   and   phrase,   and   the  finding   or   holding   of   any   such   portion   of   this   Ordinance   to   be   unconstitutional,  void,   or   ineffective   for   any   cause,   or   reason,   shall   not   affect   any   other   portion   of  this Ordinance.  § 7 — Repealer.  All   other   ordinances   or   parts   of   ordinances   inconsistent   with   this   section   are   hereby  repealed.  § 8 — Effective Date:  This   Ordinance   shall   be   in   full   force   and   effect   from   and   after   its   adoption   and   any  publication as required by law.  Susan McCartney, Mayor   Bill Rutherford, Council President  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk  Introduced:   October 8 , 2024  Adopted:   October 29 , 2024

3  2878-24  Legislative History  The   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   has   received   numerous   complaints   from  residents   in   various   neighborhoods   throughout   the   Township   regarding   parties,   events,   and/or  gatherings   wherein   the   attendees   of   the   events   have   purchased   tickets   or   wristbands   for   entry   into  residential   homes   within   the   Township.   The   purpose   of   this   Section   is   to   provide   recourse   for  the   Township   if   such   “ pop-up ”   parties   occur   in   the   future   and   to   put   the   public   on   notice   that   the  same are prohibited as a health and safety   concern for the general welfare of the public.

pages.text: 298-24  October 29, 2024  RESOLUTION   AUTHORIZING APPROVAL OF HELICOPTER LANDING AT SHP  WHEREAS,   Rutgers   University   seeks   permission   to   land   a   helicopter   and   visit   Seton  Hall   Preparatory   School   in   West   Orange   ( “ SHP ” )   between   October   22,   2024   and   December   31,  2024 ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   landing   spot   for   the   helicopter   will   be   Verducci   Field   at   SHP   (the  “ Property ” ) ,   and  WHERAS,   the   New   Jersey   Department   of   Transportatio n,   Division   of   Aeronautics ,  Inspection   &   Aircraft   Operation   does   permit   and   has   in   the   past   permitted   helicopter   landings   at  locations   in   the   Township   such   as   the   Property   when   appro ved   pursuant   to   its   application  process; and  WHEREAS,   SHP will provide   the   exact   date(s) of   the helicopter   arrival ;   and  WHEREAS,   SHP   shall   have   permission   from   the   Township   so   long   as   the   Township  Police   Department   and   Township   Fire   Department   are   advised   at   least   two   (2)   business   days   in  advance of   the helicopters   scheduled arrival ;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange ,   does   and   hereby   states   that   it   has   no   objection   to   the   issuance   of   a   Temporary  Aeronau tical   Facility   License   to   SHP ,   located   at   120   Northfield ,   West   Orange,   NJ   07052,   for   the  purpose of allowing Rutgers University and their coaching staff to visit SHP ; and it is further  RESOLVED,   that   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   for  reasonable inspection in accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale , R .M.C.   Bill Rutherford  Municipal   Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   October 22, 2024

306-24  October 29, 2024  SCHEDULE D  A RESOLUTION ACCE P TING A GRANT FROM THE   HAZARDOUS DISCHARGE SITE  REMEDIATION FUND PUBLIC ENTITY PROGRAM   THROUGH THE  NEW JERSEY ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AUTHORITY   AND THE  NEW JERSEY DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   has   applied   for   and   has   been   awarded   a   grant   in   the  amount   of   up   to   $ 44,718 .00   from   the   Hazardous   Discharge   Site   Remediation   Fund   Municipal   Grant  Program   through   the   New   Jersey   Department   of   Environmental   Protection   and   the   New   Jersey   Economic  Development   Authority   for   Preliminary   Assessment   and   Site   Investigation   along   with   report   preparation  fo r   the Biddleman, Inc. property.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   governing   body   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange   that   the   above   referenced   grant   is   hereby   accepted   by   the   Mayor   and   is   hereby   authorized   to  execute   grant   documents   as   an   authorized   representative   thereunder,   as   the   representative   for   the  Township of West Orange.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   a   comprehensive   plan   exists   specifically   for   the  development   or   redevelopment   of   contaminated   or   potentially   contaminated   real   property   in   the   host  municipality   or   that   a   realistic   opportunity   exists   that   the   Project   Si t e   will   be   developed   or   redeveloped  within a three-year period from the completion of the remediation. 307-24  Oc tober 2 9, 2024  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS ,   on   October   17 th   202 4 ,   the   Township ’ s   Director   of   Engineering   and   DPW  Supervisor   inspected   the   “ Kiss   and   Go ”   Lane   at   Kelly   Elementary   School   located   at   Standford   Avenue   to  assess   a concern regarding a broken grate for an inlet ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the   pavement   around   the   grates   and   the   inlet   grates   were   uneven   and   the   pavement  between the inlet and manhole was sunk;  WHEREAS , pursuant to the Local Public Contracts Law, Emergency Contracts, N.J.S.A.  40A:11.6a   the   Municipal   Engineer   on   October   17 th ,   202 4   notified   the   Administration   and   Purchasing  Agent  of the need to perform an emergency contract for this work; and  WHEREAS,   it   was   concluded that to expedite the work and to perform the emergency   repair of  the   roadway   to protect public health and safety ,   an   emergency contract w as   issued to   Reggio  Construction ,   Inc.   who was readily available to mobilize and perform the work ; and  WHEREAS,   Reggio   Construction, Inc.   submitted   an invoice   for the emergency repair of  the inlet   and   its associated improvements   in   the amount of $ 4, 2 00 .00 ; and  WHEREAS , the   Municipal   Engineer by letter dated   October 21 , 202 4   recommends paying the  costs of this emergency work; and  WHEREAS , sufficient funds exist for this work; and  WHEREAS , the Township Council concurs in   the foregoing . 2876-24  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER 7, TRAFFIC, SUBSECT I ON 7- 14.6   WINTER PARKING  RESTRICTIONS ,   OF THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF   THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BE IT ORDAINED,   by the Township Council of the Township of West Orange, in the County of Essex, State  of New Jersey as follows:  Section 1.   Subsection 7- 1 4.6 ,   Winter Parking Restrictions , of the Revised General Ordinances of the  Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows: ( italic type denotes deletion,   bold type  denotes addition):  Name of Street   Side   Location  Oxford Terrace   East   Entire Length  Section 2.   If any provisions of this Ordinance or ap plication thereof , under any circumstances, is  held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or applications of this Ordinance that can be given  effect without the valid provision(s) or application and to this end, the provisions of this Ordinance are severable.  Section 3.   All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are hereby  repealed.  Section 4.   This Ordinance shall take effect upo n final passage and publication   in accordance with  law   and approved b y the County of Essex, Board of Chosen Freeholders .  Susan McCartney, Mayor   Bill Rutherford, Council President  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk  Introduced:   October 8 , 2024  Adopted:   October   29 , 2024

2876-24  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance will   delete the present ban on parking on the east side of Oxford Terrace during the  period from November 15 th   and April 15 th . This change was requested by residents and approved by the  Police Chief and Municipal Engineer.   This change aligns the parking regulations on Oxford Terrace with  all the other streets in the neighborhood, which ban overnight parking between 2AM-5AM.

2879 - 24  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   3 ,   S E CTION   1 7 . 1   OF   THE   REVISED  GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ( ORDINANCES AND RESOLUTIONs   -   Preparation )  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE MUNICIPAL COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE, NEW JERSEY   that Chapter   3   o f the Revised General   Ordinances of the  Township of West Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The purpose of this ordinance is   to   require that ordinances which revise or amend  provisions of the West Orange Municipal Code be drafted in a manner to show the changes being  made to the Code under the Ordinance.  I I .   CHAPTER   3 , SECTION   1 7 . 1   SHALL BE AND HEREBY IS AMENDED AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING:  3 - 1 7 . 1   Preparation .  Ordinances and resolutions shall be prepared for the consideration of Township   Council by the  Township Attorney or the legal assistant upon motion of the Council or request of the Mayor.  Prior to introduction, each proposed ordinance requiring or permitting administrative action shall  be submitted to the Business Administrator and the department head concerned.   Ordinance  which revise or amend provisions of the West Orange Municipal Code shall be drafted in a  manner showing the revisions or amendments being made to the Code.  I II .   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any Ordinances of the Township which are in conflict with this Ordinance are hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  I V .   SEVERABILITY  If any part of this Ordinance shall be deemed invalid, such parts shall be severed and th e  invalidity thereof shall not a ffect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  Formatted:   Right

2  4848 - 4827 - 0498, v. 1  V I .   EFFECTIVE DATE  This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in accordance with  the law. pages.text: 310-24  November 12, 2024  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2   and   N.J.S.A.   24:61-45,   the   Township   Council  of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   adopted   Ordinance   26 47 -21   on   July   13,   2021,  amending the Township Code to   establish   rules and   regulations governing cannabis; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   rules   and   regulations   governing   cannabis   within   the  Township   includes   a   local   license   requirement,   which   requires   proposed   cannabis   businesses   to  apply   for   and   obtain   the   Township   Council ’ s   approval   for   the   issuance   of   a   local   license   to  operate a cannabis business within the Township of West Orange (a   “ Local License ” ); and  WHEREAS,   Dogwood   Green ,   Inc ,   an   entity   registered   to   do   business   in   the   State   of  New   Jersey,   submitted   a n   application   for   a   Local   License   for   the   proposed   operation   of   a  cannabis   retail   business   within   the   Township   at   the   property   located   at:   Block   10 ,   Lot   1.01 ,  commonly known as   5 Central Avenue   ( “ Application ” ); and  WHEREAS,   Dogwood   Green,   Inc,   has   received   site   plan   approval   from   the   Township ’ s  Planning Board for the above-referenced proposed cannabis retail business; and  WHEREAS,   Dogwood   Green,   Inc,   received   final   approval   from   the   State   of   New   Jersey  Cannabis Regulatory Commission ( “ CRC ” )   for a Class V Cannabis Retail Annual License ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   issuance   of   a   Local   License   to   Dogwood   Green,   Inc,   to   operate   as   a  Cannabis   Retail   Business   will   not   exceed   the   Township's   current   limit   of   retail   cannabis  businesses;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange ,   County   of   Essex ,   State   of   New   Jersey,   in   consideration   of   the   recommendations  of the   Chief of Police   hereby determines to be in the best interests of the Township, as follows:

2  1.   The above recitals are incorporated as though set forth at length herein.  2.   The   application   of   Dogwood   Green,   Inc,   for   a   local   license   to   operate   a   cannabis  retail   business   within   the   Township   of   West   Orange   at   the   prope rty   located   at:   Block   10,   Lot  1.01   (commonly   known   as   5   Central   Avenue ,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey),   is   in   compliance   with  the Township's local regulations on cannabis businesses   and is hereby approved.  3.   This   Resolution   shall   constitute   proof   that   said   application   complies   with   the  Township's local restrictions pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   24:6I-45(c) (1) .  4.   The   issuance   of   a   Local   License   to   operate   a   cannabis   retail   business   within   the  Township   would not exceed the Township's current limit of retail cannabis business es .  5 .   T he   Township   Clerk   is   authorized   and   directed   to   issue   a   Local   License   to  Dogwood Green, Inc .  6 .   The   Township   Clerk   shall   also   promptly   provide   Dogwood   Green,   Inc,   and   the  New Jersey Cannabis Regulatory Commission with a certified copy of this Resolution.  7 .   This Resolution shall take effect immediately.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford ,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: Nov ember 12, 2024

31 3 -24  November 12, 2024  Resolution  Authorizing Disposal of Surplus Property  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange is the owner of certain surplus property which is no  longer needed for public use; and  WHEREAS , the Township is desirous of selling said surplus property in an   “ As is ”   condition  without express or implied warranties.  NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   by the West Orange Council of the Township of  West Orange:  (1) The sale of the surplus property shall be conducted through Municibid   pursuant to State  Contract 19-GNSV1-00696 in accordance with the terms and conditions of the State Contract.  The terms and conditions of the agreement entered into with Municibid is available online at  www.municibids.com and available on the Township Website.  (2) The sale will be conducted online and the address of the auction site is   www.municibid.com  at a date and time to be determined.  (3) There is no listing fee charged to the Township.  (4) The sale will be conducted pursuant to   N.J.S.A 40A:11-36 and   Local Finance Notice   2019-  15 .  (5) A list of the surplus property is attached as   “ Exhibit A ”   hereto.  (6) The surplus property as identified shall be sold in an   “ as-is ”   condition without express or  implied warranties with the successful bidder required to execute a Hold Harmless and  Indemnification Agreement concerning use of said surplus property.  (7) The Township reserves the right to accept or reject any bid submitted.  Karen J.   Carnevale, R.M.C.   B ill Rutherford  Municipal Clerk   Council President

314-24  November 12, 2024  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   proposes   Professional   Engineering  Services for   Stop Control and All Way Stop Analysis at Various Locations ; and  WHEREAS,   for   Stop   Control   and   All   Way   Stop   Analysis   at   Various   Locations ,   the  Township requires the services of a   professional   engineering   firm; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Engineer   prepared   a   Request   for   Proposal s   dated   October  8 , 2024   for these professional services; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Engineer   solicited   a   proposal   from   six   professional   civil  engineering   firm s   specializing in traffic and transportation engineering ; and  WHEREAS,   Bright View Engineering ,   responded to the Request for Proposal; and  WHEREAS,   Bright   View   Engineering ,   ha s   performed   similar   work   for   the  Township ; and  WHEREAS,   by   letter   dated   October   29 ,   2024 ,   the   Township   Engineer   recommended  a   professional   services   contract   be   awarded   to   Bright   View   Engineering ,   70   South   Orange  Avenue   Suite   109,   Livingston,   NJ   07039 ,   for   Engineering   Services   a ssociated   with   Stop  Control and All Way Stop Analysis at Various Locations ;   and  WHEREAS,   sufficient   funds   exist   for   the   services   outlined   in   the   proposal   of   Bright  View Engineering ;   and  WHEREAS,   the Township Council concurs in the foregoing.  NOW   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the  Township   of   West   Orange,   New   Jersey   that   a   professional   services   contract   be   awarded   to  Bright   View   Engineering ,   to   provide   professional   engineering   services   for   Stop   Control   and  All Way Stop Analysis at V arious Locations   in the amount   not to exceed   $ 15,600 .00.

BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that a copy of this Resolution be published in the West  Orange Chronicle within ten (10) days of its approval as required by State Statute .  Karen   J.   Carnevale,   R.M.C. AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER www.westorange.org October 8, 2024 Trevor Taylor CME Monmouth Junction, NJ, 08852  RE:   REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS (RFP) PROFESSIONAL ENGINEERING SERVICES FOR STOP CONTROL AND ALL WAY STOP ANALYSIS AT VARIOUS LOCATIONS  Dear Mr. Taylor: The Department of Engineering for the Township of West Orange requests proposals from firms providing Engineering Services, which are capable of providing Stop Control and All Way Stop Analyses at locations to determine the most appropriate traffic control measures.  Scope of Services: I.   STOP CONTROL ANALYSIS  Consultant will conduct a field investigation at each of the following locations to evaluate existing conditions that might affect the installation of stop control at each intersection and confirming adequate sight distance is available and identify any other deficiencies within the study area. Also, Consultant is to review the available crash data attached to the RFP and available crash history from NJDOT’s Safety Voyager system to confirm if significant crash data is present. 1.   Lancaster Terrace and Manchester Road 2.   Manchester Road and Westminster Terrace 3.   Normandy Terrace and Manchester Road 4.   Cheshire Terrace and Nottingham Road 5. Cullen Drive and Carlson Road  As part of the deliverables, Consultant is to prepare a short report for each intersection which details findings and recommendations for changes including a recommendation for the installation of stop signs were appropriate consistent with the 11   th   edition of MUTCD.  II.   ALL WAY STOP ANALYSIS FOR THE INTERSECTIONS OF:     LOWELL AVE. AND MOUNTAIN WAY AND LUDDINGTON RD.     MISSISSIPPI AVE. AND MAPLE ST.  Consultant will conduct a field investigation at each location to evaluate existing intersection(s) geometry, observe traffic patterns, review available sight distance and identify other deficiencies within the study area. Also, consultant will review the available crash history from the West Orange Police Department to confirm if a significant crash history is present.   If necessary, consultant will review available crash history from NJDOT’s Safety Voyager system to confirm if significant crash data is present. The following tasks are to be conducted: A.   Perform intersection turning movement counts at the intersection(s) from 6:00 AM to 7:00 PM on a mid-week weekday (Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday) when schools are in regular session to establish existing intersection volumes. B.   Conduct a stop warrant analysis in accordance with section 2B.12 of the 11   th   Edition of the MUTCD. C.   Perform peak hour level of service analysis for the intersection(s) under existing conditions and with any recommended changes. D.   Consultant will prepare a letter report for each intersection which details findings and recommendations. E.   Include at least 1 meeting.  Proposals shall be on a lump sum basis for each task. Hourly rates shall be submitted with all proposal for any additional work. Proposals shall be submitted in writing on or before   10:00 A.M. Friday October 25, 2024   to the Department of Engineering, 25 Lakeside Avenue, West Orange, NJ 07052 and shall include a specified completion date or number of days required for completion of the work after approval is granted to proceed.   Also, evidence of proper insurance shall be submitted for approval by the Township Attorney. Very truly yours,  Zayibeth Y. Carballo  Zayibeth Y. Carballo, Director/Municipal Engineer ZYC/ag

Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  October 18, 2024  VIA EMAIL  zcarballo@westorange.org  Zayibeth Carballo, PE, PLS, PP, CME, CFM, CFS  Director / Municipal Engineer  Department of Engineering  Township of West Orange  25 Lakeside Avenue  West Orange, New Jersey 07052  973-325-4165  Re:   Request for Proposals, Professional Engineering Services For:  Stop Control and All Way Stop Analysis  West Orange Township, Essex County, New Jersey  BVE Prop# 241193  Dear Ms. Carballo:  Thank you for the opportunity to provide this Proposal for Professional Traffic Engineering  Services relating to evaluating fourteen intersections within the Township of West Orange to  recommend the installation of stop signs if appropriate as well as performing All-Way stop  analyses at three locations. This proposal is based on our review of the provided request for  proposals and our experience providing these services. Due to our familiarity with West Orange  Township and Essex County as a whole, we are confident that we can provide responsive services  at a competitive cost.  The following scope of work has been separated into phases so that it can be more easily reviewed.  The order in which the phases are presented generally follows the sequence in which the work will  be accomplished.  Section I – Scope of Services  Section II – Business Terms and Conditions  Section III – Technical Staff Hourly Rate Schedule and Reimbursable Expenses  Section IV – Client Contract Authorization

Request for Proposals:Stop Control and All Way Stop Analysis  West Orange Township, Essex County, New Jersey  BVE Prop# 241193  October 18, 2024  Page 2 of 10  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   P.O. Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  SECTION I – SCOPE OF SERVICES  TASK 1 – STOP CONTROL ANALYSES  Consistent with the information requested in the RFP, BVE will conduct an evaluation of the  following intersections to determine if the addition of stop control is appropriate:  1.   Lancaster Terrace & Manchester Road  2.   Manchester Road & Westminster Terrace  3.   Normandy Terrace & Manchester Road  4.   Cheshire Terrace & Nottingham Road  5.   Devonshire Terrace & Nottingham Road  6.   Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace  7.   Westminster Terrace & Nottingham Road  8.   Brook Place & Mount Pleasant Place  9.   Vosseler Court & Vosseler Terrace  10.   Sylvan Place & Burnett Terrace  11.   Gilbert Place & Bradford Avenue  12.   Devon Drive & Mountain Way  13.   Mountain Way South & Mountain Way  14.   Cullen Drive & Carson Road  Based on our preliminary review, each intersection is currently uncontrolled.   Our efforts will  include a visit to each location to confirm existing conditions and note any deficiencies which  should be addressed prior to any changes in traffic control, such as sight distance obstructions or  signing /striping revisions. Our evaluation will include a short letter report detailing the existing  conditions and our recommendations as appropriate in accordance with Section 2B.07 and other  applicable sections of the MUTCD. A separate report will be prepared for each intersection.

Request for Proposals:Stop Control and All Way Stop Analysis  West Orange Township, Essex County, New Jersey  BVE Prop# 241193  October 18, 2024  Page 3 of 10  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   P.O. Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  TASK 2 – ALL WAY STOP ANALYSES  Consistent with the information requested in the RFP, an All-Way stop analysis will be completed  at the following intersections to determine the most appropriate traffic control measures.  Specifically, the following intersections will be analyzed:  1.   Lowell Ave, Mountain Way & Luddington Rd  2.   Mississippi Ave & Maple St  3.   Dartmouth Rd & Winding Way  To accomplish this, BVE will undertake the following tasks:  1.   Conduct a field visit to evaluate existing intersection geometry, observe traffic patterns,  review available sight distance and identify other deficiencies within the study area.  2.   Review existing crash history based on information available from NJDOT’s Safety  Voyager system as well as crash reports provided by West Orange Township.  3.   Perform intersection turning movement counts at the intersection from 6:00 AM to 7:00  PM on a mid-week weekday (Tuesday, Wednesday or Thursday) when schools are in  regular session to establish existing intersection volumes.  4.   Conduct an All-Way stop warrant analysis in accordance with section 2B.12 of the 11 th  Edition of the MUTCD.  5.   Perform peak hour level of service analysis for the intersection under existing conditions  and with any recommended changes.  6.   Prepare a report of findings for each intersection which will include information reviewed,  results of the All-Way stop analysis and a recommendation for next steps.  7.   Attendance at one in person or virtual meeting with the Township to discuss the findings  of the reports identified above.  SCHEDULE  Work will begin upon notice to proceed; we anticipate providing our findings within four weeks  of the start of work, provided notice to proceed permits traffic counts to be scheduled within one  week of notice to proceed.

Attendance at Planning/Zoning Board meetings and other public meetings where expert testimony  is required will be billed in accordance with the Schedule of Hourly Rates in effect at the time the  meetings are held. Attendance at agency and project coordination meetings will be billed in  accordance with the Schedule of Hourly Rates in effect at the time the meetings are held. Colored  renderings, if requested for Board presentation, and meeting preparation time will be billed on an  hourly basis.  Please be reminded that traffic counts must be done when school is in normal session.  SCHEDULE OF FEES  The Fee Proposal includes all of the tasks outlined in the Scope of Services. Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  SECTION II – BUSINESS TERMS AND CONDITIONS  Bright View Engineering LLC agrees to provide professional services under the following terms and conditions:  The term Client referenced herein is the person, persons, corporation, partnership, or organization referenced in the proposal between Bright View Engineering LLC and  said Client.  1.0   SCOPE OF SERVICES:  Services not set forth in the Scope of Services, are excluded from the Scope of Services, and Bright View Engineering LLC will assume no responsibility to perform such  services under the base contract. In situations where a written contract is not executed or where additional services becomes necessary during the course of the project,  Bright View Engineering LLC may provide such services using our Technical Staff Hourly Rate Schedule in effect at the time of services. The hourly rates listed in our  Technical Staff Hourly Rate Schedule are adjusted semi-annually and the Client shall be billed at the rates that are in effect at the time of service.  Since there are substantial costs to stop and restart a project once it is underway, should a project’s progress be halted at any time by the client, for any reason, Bright  View Engineering LLC reserves the right to charge a restart fee and/or to renegotiate the remaining fees within the contract.  These Business Terms and Conditions are applicable for any additional professional services rendered for this project including, but not limited to, change orders, client  service authorization forms, etc.  2.0   STANDARD OF CARE:  In performing services, we agree to exercise professional judgment, made on the basis of the information available to us, and to use the same degree of care and skill  ordinarily exercised in similar circumstances and conditions by reputable consultants performing comparable services in the same locality. This standard of care shall be  judged as of the time the services are rendered, and not according to later standards. Reasonable people may disagree on matters involving professional judgment and,  accordingly, a difference of opinion on a question of professional judgment shall not excuse a Client from paying for services rendered. NO OTHER REPRESENTATION  OR WARRANTY, EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED, IS MADE.  3.0   INVOICES:  Bright View Engineering LLC bills its Clients on a monthly basis using a standard invoice format. This format provides for a description of services performed and a  summary of professional fees, expenses, and other charges. For more detailed invoicing requests, Bright View Engineering LLC reserves the right to charge for invoice  preparation time by staff members. Monthly invoices will be submitted based upon percentage of services completed and reimbursable expenses. Any comments or  discrepancies, relative to invoices shall be submitted in writing within fourteen (14) days or the account will be considered correct.  For professional services billed on an hourly basis, Bright View Engineering LLC reserves the right to invoice all overtime services performed by our employees using  our Technical Staff Hourly Rate Schedule in effect at the time of services at ONE AND ONE-HALF TIMES our standard hourly rate for those employees.  Expenses incurred for services, equipment, and facilities not furnished by Bright View Engineering LLC are charged to the Client at cost plus an up charge not to exceed  15 percent of the invoice for said services.  Client shall pay Bright View Engineering LLC for reimbursable expenses, including, but not limited to, application fees, printing and reproduction, courier and express  delivery service, special/overnight mailings, facsimile transmissions, specialized equipment and laboratory charges, and costs of acquiring materials specifically for the  Client. Reimbursable charges will be added to each monthly invoice and are part of Client’s responsibility.  4.0   PAYMENT:  Bright View Engineering LLC bills are payable in full UPON RECEIPT and   payment is expected within thirty (30) days . We reserve the right to assess a late charge  of 1.5 percent per month for any amounts not paid within 45 days of the billing date. In the event payment is not made according to the terms and conditions herein, the  matter may proceed to a collections agency or to an attorney for collection. Client shall be responsible for fees charged by the collections agency and/or attorney’s fees  incurred to collect the monies owed. Should the matter proceed to court, client shall also be responsible for court costs.  In addition, where payment is not received in accordance with the terms of this contract, Bright View Engineering LLC reserves the right to withdraw any applications  too federal, state, or local regulatory agencies / boards filed on behalf of the client with the understanding that these applications are the property of Bright View  Engineering LLC Bright View Engineering LLC will provide you with written notification two (2) weeks prior to taking any action to withdraw an application submitted  on behalf of the client. If payment of all outstanding invoices is not received within two (2) weeks of receipt of this letter, Bright View Engineering LLC will withdraw  all pending applications for the project.  5.0   RETAINER:  Bright View Engineering LLC reserves the right to request a retainer from the Client prior to the commencement of services on a project. While retainers are  collected prior to the start of a project, the retainer is held to the end of the project and will be applied to the final invoices. Retainers are not applied to the  beginning of the project .  6.0   RIGHT OF ENTRY/JOBSITE:  Client will provide for right of entry for Bright View Engineering LLC personnel and equipment necessary to complete our services. While Bright View Engineering  LLC will take all reasonable precautions to minimize any damage to the property, it is understood by the Client that in the normal course of our services some damage  may occur, the correction of which is not part of this Agreement.  Client shall furnish or cause to be furnished to Bright View Engineering LLC all documents and information known to the Client that relate to the identity, location,  quantity, nature, or characteristics of any hazardous or toxic substances at, on, or under the site. In addition, the Client will furnish or cause to be furnished such other  information on surface and subsurface site conditions required by Bright View Engineering LLC for proper performance of its services. Bright View Engineering LLC  shall be entitled to rely on the accuracy and completeness of Client provided documents and information in performing the services required under this Agreement and  Bright View Engineering LLC assumes no responsibility or liability for their accuracy or completeness. FEE SUMMARY  Section 1: Stop Control Analysis   (Includes 14 Intersections)   $   7, 0 00  Section   2 :   All - Way Stop Control   Analysis   (Includes   3   Intersections)   $   12 ,0 00  Section   3 : Reimbursable Expenses   Time and Materials  Section   4 :   Project Meetings and Coordination Services   Time and Materials  DESCRIPTION OF SERVICES  Section   1 :   Stop Control Analysis   (Includes 14 Intersections)  Dynamic will prepare a stop control evaluation for the fourteen (14) intersections listed below, in accordance  with the Township RFP.   This report will evaluate the Township’s   potential   change in traffic control at the  intersection s .   The existing roadway characteristics will be compared to   the guidance   set forth within   Section s  2B.09   (No Intersection Control) and 2B.11 (Minor Road Stop Control) of the Manual on Uniform Traffic  Control Devices (MUTCD), published by the Federal Highway Administration (FHA).  1.   Lancaster Terrace and Manchester Road  2.   Manchester Road and Westminster Terrace  3.   Normandy Terrace and Manchester Road  4.   Cheshire Terrace and Nottingham Road  5.   Devonshire Terrace and Nottingham Road  6.   Nottingham Road and Normandy Terrace  7.   Westminster Terrace and Nottingham Road  8.   Brook Place and Mount Pleasant Place  9.   Vosseler Ct and Vosseler Terrace  10.   Sylvan   Place and Burnett Terrace  11.   Gilbert Place and Bradford Avenue  12.   Devon Drive and Mountain Way  13.   Mountain Way S and Mountain Way  14.   Cullen Drive and Carlson Road  This   scope of work   includes the following   in accordance with the RFP:  •   A field inspection will be   conducted to obtain an inventory of existing roadway geometry, traffic control,  and location and geometry of adjacent driveways and intersections as may be gathered without an  instrument survey.  •   Field observations will be performed to observe existing traffic patterns   (vehicle & pedestrian)   and any  other traffic related deficiencies which may exist at the study locations.  •   The available sight distance for each approach to the study intersection will be observed and evaluated for  conformity with industry and jurisdictional standards.  •   An intersection crash analysis will be prepared based upon information available from NJDOT’s Safety  Voyager system as well as the crash reports provided in the Township RFP   for up to three (3) years of data ,  if available .   A schematic crash diagram illustrating the type, frequency, and relative location of crashes

will be prepared and site - specific crash trends will be compared to average statistics published by New  Jersey Department of Transportation (NJDOT) .  •   Upon completion of the study as outlined above, a   letter report   will be prepared   to document the findings  and recommendations   for each intersection.  Section   2 :   All - Way Stop Control Analysis   (Includes   3   Intersections)  Dynamic will prepare a n all - way stop control (AWSC)   evaluation for   the   three   ( 3 )   intersection s   listed below , in  accordance with the Township RFP. This report will evaluat e   the   Township’s   potential   change in   traffic control  at the intersection s .   The existing roadway characteristics and traffic volumes will be compared to the All - Way  Stop Control criteria set forth within   Section 2B.12 of   the Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices  (MUTCD), published by the Federal Highway Administration (FHA). Additionally, the impact of the   proposed  All - Way Stop Control on the operational efficiency of the intersection will be   assessed.  o   Lowell Avenue and Mountain Way and Luddington Road  o   Mississippi Avenue and Maple Street  o   Dartmouth Road and Winding Way  This   scope of work   includes the following   in accordance with the RFP:  •   A field inspection will be conducted to obtain an inventory of existing roadway geometry, traffic control,  and location and geometry of adjacent driveways and intersections as may be gathered without an  instrument survey.  •   Field observations will be performed to observe existing traffic patterns   (vehicle & pedestrian)   and any  other traffic related deficiencies which may exist at the study locations.  •   The available sight distance for each approach to the study intersection will be observed and evaluated for  conformity with industry and jurisdictional standards.  •   An intersection crash analysis will be prepared based   upon information available from NJDOT’s Safety  Voyager system as well as   police crash reports provided by   the West Orange Police Department   for up to  three (3) years of data , if available .   A schematic crash diagram illustrating the type, frequency, and relative  location of crashes will be prepared and site - specific crash trends will be compared to average statistics  published by New Jersey Department of Transportation (NJDOT) .  •   Intersection t urning movement counts will be conducted at   each intersection   during   a typical   weekday  between the hours   of 6:00 AM and 7:00 PM.   The   counts will be conducted on   either   a Tuesday,  Wednesday, or Thursday   when school s   are in regular session to establish existing intersection volumes.  •   The   intersection conditions (including the traffic volumes & crash history)   will be compared to the all - way  stop control criteria contained within the   11 th   edition of the   MUTCD.  •   Capacity analyses will be conducted for the weekday morning and weekday evening peak hours to  determine existing and projected peak hour capacities and to define existing and projected levels of service .  Any recommended changes will be reflected in the analysis.  •   Upon completion of the study as outlined above, a   letter   report   will be prepared   to document the findings  and recommendatio ns   for each intersection

•   Reimbursable expenses associated with initial preparation and submission of the report is included within  this task.   Subsequent reimbursable expenses that may be required will be billed on a time and materials  basis in accordance with the enclosed rate sc hedule.  •   Attendance at one (1) meeting is included within this task. Subsequent meetings that may be required will  be billed on a time and materials basis in accordance with the enclosed rate schedule.  Section   3 :   Reimbursable Expenses  Reimbursable costs and expenses incurred by Dynamic   Traffic   in connection with the   work outlined in   S ection s  1   and 2   are included in the lump sum fee. Any other reimbursables   including but not limited to postage, travel  expenses, printing and mileage will be invoiced in accordance with the attached Dynamic Traffic’s   Professional  Services Rate Schedule .  Section   4 :   Project   Meetings and Coordination Services  The intensity, methods and frequency of communications required for a project varies from client to client and  project to project, but is essential to expediting the project successfully. As it is not always possible to determine  the scope of work required   for this phase prior to the commencement of work, this firm will provide an estimated  scope of work as follows:  •   Conference calls  •   Meetings and generation of Meeting Memorandums  •   Correspondence and coordination of governmental agencies  •   Review and address agency review letters  •   Preparation   for meetings or hearings  Attendance at one (1) meeting is included within   Sections 2   a s noted above.   All additional   meetings,  consultations and correspondence as noted above will be billed in accordance with the hourly rate schedule as  included herein.  Project Schedule  It is anticipated that the   Stop Control Analysis and   All   Way Stop Control   Analysis   ( S ection s   1   and 2 )   can be  completed within   eight   ( 8 )   weeks   ( 56   days)   from receipt of   the   “notice to proceed” from the Township .   Note it  is highly recommended that the traffic counts be performed while local schools are in regular session. Depending  on when the notice to proceed is issued relative to the school   calendar , there could be impacts to the project  schedule.  Notes and Assumptions  This proposal is limited to the scope of services specifically defined herein.   Any additional services or out - of -  scope work items that are requested or are determined to be necessary will be invoiced in accordance with  Dynamic Traffic’s   Professional Services Rate Schedule   enclosed herein .

FIRM PROFILE  Dynamic   Traffic,   LLC   provides   transportation   planning   and   design   services   for   numerous municipalities and counties throughout   the state. Our   design   capabilities include intersection improvements, roadway widening, traffic signal and electrical plan design, complete streets designs, traffic calming designs, pedestrian safety designs, bicycle facility designs, MUTCD compliance and all   aspects   of   signing   and   striping.   Principals   of   Dynamic   Traffic   have   recently   provided transportation, planning and design services for municipalities including Maplewood, Neptune, Spring Lake Heights, Elizabeth, North Caldwell, Summit, Ringwood, Asbury Park and many others as well as Mercer, Ocean, Monmouth, Essex, Morris, Somerset, Camden, and Atlantic counties. Our strengths lie in the relationships we have forged over numerous years of working in both the private and public sectors, which gives us a unique perspective and results in our focus on implementing improvements.  Dynamic Traffic, LLC   is a privately held corporation established and incorporated in 2012 which consists of a team of licensed professionals led by a team of 9 principals supported by 17 staff/engineers working in numerous municipalities, counties, and states. Communication with the client and stakeholders is critical to the success of a project and Dynamic Traffic, LLC will ensure complete communication with the client along with diligent follow-up. Our unique attention to customer service and client satisfaction will help you meet the Township’s objectives. Our focus on providing traffic engineering services at a superior value will also help to ensure your project is successful and within your budget. Traffic Services include:  •   Traffic Impact Studies  •   Corridor Studies  •   Intersection Control and Design  •   Traffic Signal Design  •   Electrical Plan Design  •   Signing and Striping Design  •   Preparation of Traffic Control Plans  •   Traffic Signal Timing/Optimization  •   Parking Studies (restrictions, demand, and pricing)  •   Crash Analyses  •   Traffic Count Program Design and Implementation  •   Traffic Signal Warrant Analyses  •   Traffic Reviews  •   Accessible Curb Ramp Design  •   Geometric Design and Layout  •   Multi-Way Stop Warrant Analysis For all the items above, John McCormack, PE, PTOE will be the key contact person leading our bench of professionals to support these tasks as necessary. Dynamic Traffic (and all principals of the firm) maintains the proper professional engineering licensure and remains in good standing. Neither the firm nor any individuals assigned to this engagement are suspended, or otherwise prohibited from professional practice by any federal, state, or local agency. Our firm has a proven track record in preparing engineering studies and other documents for public clients to serve various needs and carrying that work into fully designed plans. We look forward to continuing to use this experience to benefit the Township of West Orange.

October 2018) Department of the Treasury Internal Revenue Service  Request for Taxpayer Identification Number and Certification  a   Go to   www.irs.gov/FormW9   for instructions and the latest information. Give Form to the requester. Do not send to the IRS. Print or type.  See   Specific Instructions   on page 3.  1   Name (as shown on your income tax return). Name is required on this line; do not leave this line blank.  2   Business name/disregarded entity name, if different from above  3   Check appropriate box for federal tax classification of the person whose name is entered on line 1. Check only   one   of the following seven boxes. Individual/sole proprietor or single-member LLC C Corporation   S Corporation   Partnership   Trust/estate Limited liability company. Enter the tax classification (C=C corporation, S=S corporation, P=Partnership)   a  Note:   Check the appropriate box in the line above for the tax classification of the single-member owner. Do not check LLC if the LLC is classified as a single-member LLC that is disregarded from the owner unless the owner of the LLC is another LLC that is   not   disregarded from the owner for U.S. federal tax purposes. Otherwise, a single-member LLC that is disregarded from the owner should check the appropriate box for the tax classification of its owner. Other (see instructions)   a  4   Exemptions (codes apply only to certain entities, not individuals; see instructions on page 3): Exempt payee code (if any) Exemption from FATCA reporting code (if any)  (Applies to accounts maintained outside the U.S.)  5   Address (number, street, and apt. or suite no.) See instructions.  6   City, state, and ZIP code Requester’s name and address (optional)  7   List account number(s) here (optional)  Part I   Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN)  Enter your TIN in the appropriate box. The TIN provided must match the name given on line 1 to avoid backup withholding. For individuals, this is generally your social security number (SSN). However, for a resident alien, sole proprietor, or disregarded entity, see the instructions for Part I, later. For other entities, it is your employer identification number (EIN). If you do not have a number, see   How to get a TIN,   later.  Note:   If the account is in more than one name, see the instructions for line 1. Also see   What Name and Number To Give the Requester   for guidelines on whose number to enter.  Social security number –   – or Employer identification number – Part II   Certification  Under penalties of perjury, I certify that: 1. The number shown on this form is my correct taxpayer identification number (or I am waiting for a number to be issued to me); and 2. I am not subject to backup withholding because: (a) I am exempt from backup withholding, or (b) I have not been notified by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) that I am subject to backup withholding as a result of a failure to report all interest or dividends, or (c) the IRS has notified me that I am no longer subject to backup withholding; and 3. I am a U.S. citizen or other U.S. person (defined below); and 4. The FATCA code(s) entered on this form (if any) indicating that I am exempt from FATCA reporting is correct.  Certification instructions. Carballo  Director/Municipal Engineer Township of West Orange 25 Lakeside Avenue West Orange, NJ 07052 RE:   Proposal to Provide Professional Engineering Services for Stop Control and All-Way Stop Analysis at Various Locations Township of West Orange, Essex County, NJ Dear Ms. Carballo: This scope of services is relative to the above-referenced analyses as per the Township’s RFP dated October 8, 2024. The following tasks will be performed as a part of the traffic study:  I.   STOP CONTROL ANALYSIS  a.   Field View – A field view will be performed at each of the fourteen (14) study intersection locations to observe existing road and traffic conditions.   Notations of existing physical characteristics of the road system (e.g., road/lane widths, parking, speed limits and other signing, sight distances/restrictions, lighting, crash debris, etc.) will be made. Photographs (digital) of each approach will be collected as a part of the field view. The fourteen intersections are listed as follows: 1.   Lancaster Terrace and Manchester Road 2.   Manchester Road and Westminster Terrace 3.   Normandy Terrace and Manchester Road 4.   Cheshire Terrace and Nottingham Road 5.   Devonshire Terrace and Nottingham Road 6.   Nottingham Road and Normandy Terrace 7.   Westminster Terrace and Nottingham Road 8.   Brook Place and Mount Pleasant Place 9.   Vosseler Ct and Vosseler Terrace 10. Sylvan Place and Burnett Terrace 11. Gilbert Place and Bradford Ave 12. Devon Drive and Mountain Way 13. Mountain Way S and Mountain Way 14. Cullen Drive and Carlson Road b.   Traffic Data – Classified intersection turning movement traffic counts will be collected at each of the fourteen (14) study intersection locations. Counts are needed to determine which intersection approaches have the higher traffic volumes (and thus are the predominant movements).

Zayibeth Y. Carballo   October 25, 2024  Director/Municipal Engineer  Page | 2  -   Counts will extend from 7:00 to 9:00 AM and from 4:00 to 6:00 PM on a typical Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday.  -   Count Classification:     Motorcycles     Cars & Light Goods     Buses     Single Unit Trucks     Articulated Trucks     Pedestrians & Bicycles in the crosswalk c.   Review Crash Data – Available crash data attached to the RFP and available crash history from NJDOT’s Safety Voyager system (last 3 full years of available information) will be reviewed to confirm if significant crash data is present. The review will identify trends identifying the predominant crash types at each intersection. Crashes will not be plotted or analyzed. d.   Prepare Technical Memorandum – A Technical Memorandum will be prepared to include a discussion of each intersection. Information outlined will include:  -   Field view observations  -   Traffic counts (summarized by peak hour and graphically plotted)  -   Findings and recommendations consistent with the 2023 (11   th   edition) MUTCD  II.   ALL WAY STOP WARRANT ANALYSIS  An All-Way STOP Warrant Analysis will be performed in accordance with the new 2023 MUTCD guidelines (11 th Edition) for the three (3) intersections listed below:  -   Lowell Avenue and Mountain Way and Luddington Road  -   Mississippi Avenue and Maple Street  -   Dartmouth Road and Winding Way Work will include the following: a.   Field View – A field view will be performed at each of the three (3) study intersection locations to observe existing road and traffic conditions. Notations of existing physical characteristics of the road system (e.g., road/lane widths, parking, speed limits and other signing, sight distances/restrictions, lighting, crash debris, etc.) will be made. Photographs (digital) of each approach will be collected as a part of the field view. b.   Review Available Crash History – GPI will review recent crash history (last 3 full years of available information) obtained from the West Orange Police Department to confirm if significant crash history is present. NJ Safety Voyager will also be utilized to review crash history for most recent full three (3) years of available crash records. The review will identify trends regarding the predominant crash types at each intersection. Crashes will not be plotted or analyzed. c.   Traffic Data – Classified intersection turning movement traffic counts will be collected at each of the three (3) study intersection locations.  -   Counts will extend from 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM on a typical Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday when schools are in regular session.

Zayibeth Y. Carballo   October 25, 2024  Director/Municipal Engineer  Page | 3  -   Count Classification:     Motorcycles     Cars & Light Goods     Buses     Single Unit Trucks     Articulated Trucks     Pedestrians & Bicycles in the crosswalk d.   All Way Stop Warrant Analysis – As noted on pages 78 – 79 of 1161 of the 2023 MUTCD, the following warrants will be evaluated:  •   Warrant A: Crash Experience – It is assumed that GPI will review the crash summary information for Years 2021, 2022, and 2023 collected through NJDOT’s Safety Voyager database, as well as the individual crash records collected from the West Orange Police Department. Crash diagrams will not be prepared as they are not required for the Warrant Analysis.  •   Warrant B: Sight Distance – We will obtain sight distance calculations for each approach.   Actual field conditions will be taken into account as on-street parking may obstruct sight distances. GPI will then apply this information to this warrant analysis.  •   Warrant C: Transition to Signal Control or Transition to Yield Control at a Circular Intersection – It is assumed that this warrant is not applicable since there are currently no arrangements being made for the installation of a traffic control signal, and this intersection is not circular in nature.  •   Warrant D: 8-Hour Volume (Vehicles, Pedestrians, Bicycles) – The traffic data as summarized in Task II.c. (above) will be utilized to perform this warrant. Charts and/or tables will be prepared as required.  •   Warrant E: Other Factors – Engineering judgement will be utilized to determine if any other conditions may exist to warrant the need for All-Way STOP traffic control at these intersections. e.   Levels of Service (LOS) Analyses – LOS analyses will be performed for the weekday peak traffic hours (AM and PM peak traffic periods) for the three (3) intersections based on policies and guidance as contained in the Highway Capacity Manual (HCM) 7   th   Edition. Results of the analyses will be presented in tabular form. f.   Technical Memorandum – A draft Technical Memorandum (Report of Findings) will first be prepared and presented to the Township that summarizes the work associated with this study. Recommendations for next steps will be included, if and as applicable.   A final Technical Memorandum incorporating any comments received from the Township will then be prepared and submitted. g.   Project Management, QA/QC, and Meeting Attendance - It is assumed that this assessment will be completed within four (4) months of notice to proceed. It is assumed that GPI will attend up to one (1) virtual meeting with the Township to review and present the results of this assessment. NOTE: This proposal does not include the identification for the type, size, and/or location of any signs or pavement markings. This work would have to be performed as a part of a separate design project.  FEE PROPOSAL  The fee proposal associated with this technical scope of work is attached.
