1  AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER  www.westorange.org  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  DEPARTMENT OF ENGINEERING  25 Lakeside Avenue, West Orange, New Jersey 07052  SUSAN McCartney  MAYOR  ZAYIBETH CARBALLO   Tel: (973) 325 - 41 60  Director/Municipal Engineer  Email:   zcarballo@westorange.org  October 11 ,   2024  Trevor Taylor  CME  379 US Route 1 South, Suite 100  Monmouth Junction, NJ, 08852  RE:   REQUEST   FOR   PROPOSALS  OSKAR SCHINDLER PERFORMING ARTS CENTER (OSPAC)  AND CRYSTAL LAKE IMPROVEMENTS  Dear Mr. T ayl or   ,  The Department of Engineering for the Township of West Orange requests proposals  from firms providing both Civil Engineering and Land Surveying Services, which are capable  of preparing the necessary permitting applications, concept plan, preliminary and construction  plans,   specifications   and   bid   documents   for   improvements   at   OSPAC   and   Crystal   Lake.  Township intends to improve OSPAC and Crystal Lake by creating additional amenities to  better utilize the area around it.   The project construction cost will be partially funded by the  American Rescue Plan (ARPA) for which the funds will need to be expended by December  2025. As a result, the project schedule must be on an expediated process to meet the anticipated  date.  The Township envisions the improvements to OSPAC consisting of furnishing and  installing a sound system for the amphitheater including the installation of ADA accessible  bleachers on concrete pads. Crystal Lake improvements will consist of the reconstruction of  approximately 0.5 miles of stone dust and/or HMA pathway around the lake between 6 ft. to 8  ft. wide including the installation of decorative pathway LED lighting, electronic entrance LED  sign, benches, and trash receptacles. Other possible amenities might include the construction of  a fishing pier and floating dock, paddle boats and canoes launch area, prefab pedestrian bridge,  the construction of a small shoreline with outdoor showers, seating area pavilion, a playground,  and a concession stand.

2  AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER  www.westorange.org  The project might also include retrofitting certain storm drain inlets along drains in the  vicinity of the lake to meet standards outlined in the Stormwater Management rules. The  retrofitting focuses on solids and floatable control to help reduce the amounts of pollutants that  may enter the stormwater system that impact receiving waters.  Crystal Lake is a spring-fed lake and the headwater to the Rahway River and a passive  park facility that is underutilized by West Orange residents. According to FEMA’s Flood  Insurance Rate Map (FIRM) Map # 34013C0111F with an effective date of June 4, 2007,  Crystal Lake is within Zone A flood hazard area. Please see attached FIRM map and Stream  Stat for drainage area to the Crystal Lake. PRE-APPLICATION REQUEST SUBMITTALS  It is recommended that every request for a pre-application meeting include the items listed below. Please note that each Rule implemented by the Division of Land Use Regulation has specific requirements for pre-application meetings. Therefore, any pre- application meeting request that includes items as noted in the applicable rule will also be accepted.  1)   A complete written description of the project and all proposed activities (the State Plane coordinates for the site should be included in this document). 2)   A written meeting agenda with goals and objectives. 3)   One copy of a USGS quad map with the site clearly outlined to scale. 4)   One copy of the municipal tax map with the site clearly outlined to scale. 5)   One color copy of an aerial photograph of the site. 6)   One copy of the appropriate United States Soil Conservation Service map(s) with the site clearly outlined to scale. 7)   One copy of each previous approval received from the Department concerning the site, if such approvals exist. 8)   One set of color photographs depicting the entire project area, mounted on 8.5” x 11” paper and accompanied by a map showing the location and direction from which each photograph was taken. 9)   One set of drawings depicting the proposed project.  Special Notes  1)   If applicable, the potential applicant shall also include a copy of any floodplain mapping such as a Department flood hazard area map or FEMA flood insurance map with the site clearly outlined to scale. TABLE OF CONTENTS  TITLE   SECTION  Cover Letter  Technical Proposal   1 Key Personnel   2  Organization Chart Resumes  Evidence of Insurance   3
SECTION 1 – TECHNICAL PROPOSAL

1  PROJECT UNDERSTANDING & APPROACH, SCOPE OF SERVICES, AND FEES  PROJECT UNDERSTANDING & APPROACH Matrix New World Engineering, Land Surveying and Landscape Architecture, PC (Matrix) has successfully executed and delivered on multiple concurrent large-scale and complex contracts for public agencies (Federal, State, and County), private corporations, architects, engineering firms, and commercial and residential developers. By providing full-service engineering and landscape architectural services that range from preliminary site assessment and investigation to conceptual layout and design to development of detailed site drawings and construction specifications, Matrix combines architectural design skills with the scientific principles of engineering and the broad development knowledge of urban planning to develop aesthetic and practical relationships with the land. Client satisfaction is always our main goal and thus we ensure that staff availability and qualifications are never a compromise. A primary contact and project manager will provide a direct link between Matrix personnel and the Township of West Orange (Township) for consistent direction and collaboration, but the entirety of Matrix’s staff and management are available when needed or warranted. Our current workload is steady yet flexible. We prefer to maintain control over our growth and make sure we are never over- or under-staffed. We presently have sufficient capacity to give the Township our undivided attention to meet project goals and deadlines, and we do not anticipate any change to this for the duration of the project. We also plan to utilize the same managers and staff throughout the project to ensure consistency. Matrix acknowledges the schedule that was outlined in the RFP. We will make every effort to meet the schedule and notify the Township if we anticipate any deviations from the schedule. It is our understanding that the Township wishes to improve the area around Crystal Lake and provide some upgrades to the Oskar Schindler Performing Arts Center (OSPAC) space. The elements that may be incorporated into the design are as follows:  Crystal Lake     Reconstruct/repair the approximately half mile walking path around the perimeter of the lake     Provide lighting for the walking path. Due to the shallow bedrock visible at the site, we recommend providing low voltage lighting (bollard type) for the walkway to limit the amount of excavation that would be required.     Provide additional site benches and trash receptacle along the walking path.     Locate a small, prefabricated shade structure with seating. We recommend this is located at the paver plaza area to take advantage of the views across the lake.     Locate and design a floating dock that could be used for fishing and boating on the lake. The dock should be located at the paver plaza area. A small concrete wall already exists at the edge of the lake in this area.     Replacement of the small pedestrian bridge. This could be potentially eliminated by routing pedestrians to the sidewalk along Boland Drive.

2  Oskar Schindler Performing Arts Center     Design and placement of speakers for the existing sound system.     Provide an LED type entrance sign. It is our understanding that the Township will select a sign that our team will locate and provide power to.     Provide for barrier free seating areas for the outdoor amphitheater space.     Recommend an area for the location of a food truck.     Design a small playground area if possible. SCOPE OF SERVICES The scope of services is described in detail below:  PHASE 1 - FIELD INVESTIGATION/RECONNAISSANCE ITEM 1:   KICK OFF MEETING AND EXISTING CONDITIONS  Matrix will attend a kick-off meeting with the Township to discuss the goals for the park and gather readily available information about the park from the Township. This will include an initial discussion on the type and number of facilities and the construction budget for potential park improvements. This will also be the time to discuss potential areas of concern and specific areas in need of review prior to visiting the site. As part of this item, Matrix will review the available information to fully understand the conditions as they are today, which will set the foundation for the creation of the concept plan for the park. In addition, Matrix staff will visit the site to evaluate any areas of concern brought to our attention by the Township and to assess the overall condition of the park space. Matrix will attend up to two (2) meetings as part of this task.  FIXED FEE: $2,500.00 ITEM 2:   WETLANDS DELINEATION  Matrix will perform a field delineation to establish the jurisdictional limits of existing wetlands and open waters on the project site. The delineation will be conducted consistent with the   1989 Federal Manual for Identifying and Delineating Jurisdictional Wetlands   which is the methodology required pursuant to the Freshwater Wetlands Protection Act Rules (NJAC 7:7A). The wetland boundary will be marked with numbered flagging placed at 50- to 75-foot intervals. We will also record soil, hydrologic, and vegetation data at intervals along the wetland boundary and take photographs at representative locations.  FIXED FEE: $7,000.00 ITEM 3:   SURVEYING SERVICES  Matrix understands the area required to provide land surveying services in support of the above referenced project and as shown in Fig.1 below to be as described as follows.

3  Fig.1 Approximate Survey Area  Topographic Survey  Matrix will schedule on-site work with the project manager from your office who will work directly with facility management. Matrix will need full access to the property with parking available for their vehicles if convenient public parking is unavailable.     Matrix will prepare a Base Map utilizing aerial orthographic photographs downloaded from an on-line source with field data to supplement planimetric features plus tax map parcel data to be provided by the Township in CAD for the preparation of Concept Plans;     Matrix will provide a Boundary and Topographic for OSPAC and Crystal Lake for the preparation of permitting and construction plans;     Matrix will at a minimum map the location of wetlands, buffers, riparian zones, limits of the 100- Year Flood Hazard Area (FHA) and top of bank location as per Professional Wetlands Scientist and Engineer;     Location of tree canopy around the lake will be established during survey field operations;

4     Sufficient topographic information will be provided for the design of proposed curb ramps and ADA accessible areas;     Matrix will establish horizontal control based on North American Datum 1983 (New Jersey State Plane Coordinate System-NAD 83) and North American Vertical Datum 1988 (NAVD 88), utilizing GNSS static methods and closed loop traverses;     Benchmark(s) to be set on site for future use;     Matrix will establish the water surface elevation of Crystal Lake.     Matrix will provide an Existing Conditions/Survey Control plan;     As determined in consultation with the Project Engineer and the Township of West Orange as needed, Matrix will coordinate utility mark-outs Matrix will map all surface features including all existing elevations, high and low points, contour lines at 1-ft-intervals, elevations and street grades, utility casting, manholes, fire hydrants, catch basins and invert elevations of catch basins, pipes and drainage structures (e.g., size, material, type, rim/grate elevation, and invert elevations at the inlet and outlet), existing site furniture (including but not limited to fences, sign poles, benches, tables, light and utility poles, etc.), tree centerline, diameter and breast height (DBH) and drip line locations of trees 6” DBH and above, tree stump centerlines and diameters, tree pit extents, driveways, curb cuts, crosswalks, ramps, stairwells, existing buildings and structures. Matrix will utilize conventional land survey, plus possible combinations of 3D laser scanning and SLAM LiDAR technologies to perform this survey. Conventional survey equipment will also be used for establishing survey control and collecting topography in shadow areas not included in the 3D laser scan. Matrix will use Leica GNSS equipment to establish benchmarks and horizontal survey control on site. Matrix will map underground utilities, engaging the services of a subsurface utility provider (GPR) is excluded, to obtain Level B SUE survey per ASCE Standard Guideline for the Collection and Depiction of Existing Subsurface Utility data in areas designated by the Project Engineer. Matrix will also depict Water and Sewer mapping on the survey.  Exclusions and assumptions made for this proposal:     The Client will provide Matrix with the existing topographic, property boundary, and utility mapping or information if pertinent to the project.     It will be the responsibility of the Client to provide and coordinate the scheduled access to the site for Matrix staff as needed.     Matrix will provide deliverables as specified in the RFP. Matrix will provide all electronic copies in Civil3D AutoCAD 2024 format (or other AutoCAD version if required).     Soft-Dig is not included in this proposal.     Construction stakeout/layout is not included in this proposal.     The proposal does not include TV scanning of the storm and sanitary lines.     The proposal does not include cleaning of the storm/sanitary lines.     The proposal does not include a designated tree survey.     The proposal does not include wetland delineation services under the survey task.     Matrix will locate wetland delineation markers set by others.

5  “Service delays associated with any acts of war, acts of God, acts of a legislative, administrative, or judicial entity, or other forms of Force Majeure, including but not limited to fires, floods, hurricanes, unusually severe weather, disease, and pandemic, will not constitute grounds for default. It is expressly recognized that any of these events may result in disruptions of work due to restrictions on access to legal documents, record plans, and work sites. Based upon the effect of the Force Majeure on the performance of the Work, Matrix will be granted a time extension and compensated for time expended pursuant to its good faith attempts to achieve the Work. (Client’s) remedy for any delay caused by Force Majeure will be the negotiation of an equitable adjustment of the project schedule.”   It will be the responsibility and expense of the client to supply Matrix with access to the subject and adjoining properties and/or permits along with traffic safety control if required by any State, County, local authority, or the Township. This will also include the additional cost for safety personnel and/or police presence. No work will be performed outside the limits of the subject property boundary if the above- mentioned property access, highway access, and/or traffic safety is required and not provided to Matrix. A separate authorization from the client will be requested prior to beginning any field work if traffic safety is required. Any delays to the field or office work, caused by not having proper access to the site or adjoining properties, and/or for traffic safety, the client will then be invoiced in accordance with the current Matrix fee schedule.  FIXED FEE: $18,000.00 PHASE 2 - DESIGN ITEM 1:   CONCEPT PLAN  Upon completion of Phase 1 Items 1, 2 and 3, Matrix will review the data found in our field investigation and create one concept plan for the project that will incorporate as many of the requested program elements as possible. Due to the nature of the site, not all the desired improvements may be possible to construct in this location. Matix will analyze the best locations for the improvements based on the results of the tasks above. A color rendered concept plan will be presented to the Township for review and comment. This will be the basis for the discussion with the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (NJDEP) at the preapplication meeting (see item 2 below). An initial probable cost of construction will also be provided to the Township at this stage to verify adequate funding is available for the improvement shown. As part of this item Matrix will attend up to one (1) meeting with the Township’s staff.  FIXED FEE: $6,500.00 ITEM 2:   NJDEP PREAPPLICATION MEEETING AND PREPARATION  In order to establish regulatory framework required to obtain state environmental permits for regulated activities presented within the developed concept plan. Matrix will schedule and participate in one on- line pre-application meeting with NJDEP to present the project, obtain initial agency feedback and discuss permitting requirements. Comments from The NJDEP will be discussed with the Township and if any changes are required to the concept plan, they will be addressed in the construction document item of this proposal.  FIXED FEE: $4,200.00

This proposal also includes time for one (1) site visit by a project engineer for with review and confirmation of the existing utility locations. Our fee includes normal coordination with the Township Department of Engineering during development of the plans. This proposal does not include design or permitting associated with off-site utility services or with building construction. 7  ITEM 4:   PERMITTING AND APPROVALS  Based on the RFP, Matrix anticipates the following permits and approvals will be required for the project:  Letter Of Interpretation (LOI) Application  In order to obtain state confirmation of the field delineated limits of jurisdictional wetlands within the boundary of the site, Matrix will prepare and submit to the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection, Division of Land Resource Protection an application for a Letter of Interpretation (LOI) to verify the extent of wetlands, open waters, and wetland transition areas on the site. The LOI application will be prepared in accordance with the Freshwater Wetlands Protection Act Rules in effect as of the date of this proposal. Once issued, the LOI will remain valid for a period of five years from the date of issuance.  FIXED FEE: $4,500.00  NJDEP Freshwater Wetlands Protection Act Permit Application  As noted within the request for proposals (RFP), proposed improvements will likely require state approval of multiple Statewide General Permits under the Freshwater Wetlands Protection Act Rules. Permits may include minor outfalls and intake structures (GP 11), Trails and Boardwalks (GP 17) Docks and piers (GP 19), bank stabilization (GP 20), above ground utility lines (GP 21), and minor channel or stream cleaning for government agencies (GP 25). As such, Matrix will prepare and electronically submit to NJDEP an application for up to six (6) Statewide General Permit under a single permit application submission. Also, if required, Matrix will include an application for a transition area waiver to allow minor encroachments into the wetland transition areas. The application will be prepared and submitted consistent with the requirements set forth within the Freshwater Wetlands Protection Act Rules in effect as of the date of this proposal. It is assumed that the proposed activities will comply with the requirements of the Statewide General Permit program and transition area waivers. Therefore, an individual wetlands permit will not be required and is excluded from this scope of work. It is also assumed that the disturbance to the wetlands/waters will not require mitigation.  FIXED FEE: $18,500.00  NJDEP Flood Hazard Area (FHA) Individual Permit Application (if required)  As noted within the RFP, the site is subject to a 300 foot wide Riparian Zone regulated under the New Jersey Flood Hazard Area Control Act. Additionally, based on a review of available background mapping, a portion of the site is located within the flood hazard area of a tributary to the Passaic River. If desired improvements are proposed within the flood hazard area or riparian zone of Crystal Lake do not meet FHACA Permit-by-Rule criteria, then prior NJDEP approval pursuant to the Flood Hazard Area Control Act Rules (N.J.A.C. 7:13) will be required. Therefore, if needed, under this item Matrix will prepare and electronically submit to NJDEP an application for a flood hazard area individual permit. The application will be prepared and submitted consistent with the requirements set forth within the Flood Hazard Area Control Act Rules (Rules) in effect as of the date of this proposal. The flood hazard area design flood elevation will be determined using the higher of either Method 1 (DEP Flood Study) or Method 3 (FEMA fluvial method) as outlined in the Rules. It is assumed that activities within regulated areas will comply with the requirements of the Rules. Therefore, an application for a hardship exception from any portion of the Rules is excluded from this scope of work. It is also assumed that disturbance to regulated areas will not exceed any threshold requiring mitigation.  FIXED FEE: $19,500.00

Newark Bay Wetlands Restoration, City of Newark, NJ –   Practice Lead responsible for the implementation of a coastal wetland restoration project funded by a grant made to the City of Newark by the Department of Interior National Fish and Wildlife Foundation (NFWF) through the Hurricane Sandy Coastal Resiliency Competitive Grant Program.   The project entails the restoration and protection of a 12-acre wetlands complex located along Newark Bay.   The site, which was historically separated from Newark Bay by an 800-foot revetment wall, is one of only a few remaining natural areas remaining in this highly-industrialized portion of Newark Bay. Although some healthy tidal wetlands remain, most of the site has become highly degraded by invasive species and severe shoreline erosion. This project will restore, enhance, and protect the wetland complex through the implementation of multiple resilient and sustainable measures.   These include a living shoreline, saltmarsh restoration, and invasive species eradication.   The project will ultimately buffer the wetland complex from continued erosion from the tidal action of Newark Bay, improve flood control for the adjoining upland areas in the City of Newark, and restore the natural habitat. 1460 Route 9 South Howell, NJ, 07731 732.462.7400 www.cmeusa1.com October 24, 2024  Sent Via Electronic and Regular Mail  Zayibeth Carballo, Director / Municipal Engineer Township of West Orange 66 Main Street West Orange, NJ 07052  Re:   Estimate of Fees for Professional Engineering Services  Oskar Schindler Performing Arts Center and Crystal Lake Improvements Township of West Orange, Essex County, NJ  Dear Ms. Carballo: We are pleased to present our estimate of fees for the design, permitting and construction phase services for the above-referenced project. It is our understanding that the Township is seeking to improve the Okar Schindler Performing Arts Center (OSPAC) and Crystal Lake by creating additional amenities and better utilize the park’s area.   The Township envisions the improvements to OSPAC consisting of furnishing and installing a sound system for the amphitheater including the installation of ADA accessible bleachers on concrete pads. Crystal Lake improvements will consist of the reconstruction of approximately 0.5 miles of stone dust and/or HMA pathway around the lake between 6 ft. to 8 ft. wide including the installation of decorative pathway LED lighting, electronic entrance LED sign, benches, and trash receptacles. Other possible amenities might include the construction of a fishing pier and floating dock, paddle boats and canoes launch area, prefab pedestrian bridge, the construction of a small shoreline with outdoor showers, seating area pavilion, a playground, and a concession stand. The project might also include retrofitting certain storm drain inlets along drains in the vicinity of the lake to meet standards outlined in the Stormwater Management rules. The retrofitting focuses on solids and floatable control to help reduce the amounts of pollutants that may enter the stormwater system that impact receiving waters. Crystal Lake is a spring-fed lake and the headwater to the Rahway River and a passive park facility that is underutilized by West Orange residents. According to FEMA’s Flood Insurance Rate Map (FIRM) Map # 34013C0111F with an effective date of June 4, 2007, Crystal Lake is within Zone A flood hazard area.

Ms. Zayibeth Carballo   October 24, 2024 Township of West Orange Re:   Professional Engineering Services   Page 4 Oskar Schindler Performing Arts Center and Crystal Lake Improvements  S:\West Orange\Proposals\24-10-24 West Orange OSCPA and Crystal Lake.docx  Flood Hazard Area Control Act Rules (FHA) The proposed improvements are near and within the regulated areas of the Crystal Lake. Crystal Lake is considered a FW2-NT water (according to the rules found within the Surface Water Quality standards found at N.J.A.C. 7:9B) and as a result will require a 50-foot riparian zone (as found at N.J.A.C. 7:13-4.1). Additionally, Crystal Lake is mapped as a FEMA Flood Zone A with no base flood elevations determined. Given the proposed improvements may take place within the riparian zone or within the flood hazard area, various Flood Hazard Area Permits may be required including but not limited to, Permit-by-Rules, General Permits, or an Individual Permit. If the project is considered a major development in terms of stormwater, as in there will be more than 1 acre of ground disturbance or more than ¼ acre of new impervious surface, an Individual Permit will be required. Once the concept plan is developed, the specific permits required will be identified and discussed with the NJDEP during a pre-application meeting. Based on a preliminary review of USGS StreamStats, Crystal Lake appears to drain less than 50 acres and therefore would not have a flood hazard area associated with it (in accordance with N.J.A.C. 7:13-2.3). CME will confirm these findings and discuss with the NJDEP during the pre-application meeting. CME will prepare and submit all required information for a NJDEP Permit application based on   the   final   design.   This   includes   preparing   public   notices,   an   Environmental   Report demonstrating compliance with the regulations, permit plans, and a stormwater/design report where necessary. It is understood that the permit fees are the responsibility of the Township. Freshwater Wetland Protection Act Rules While freshwater wetlands are not mapped on site, it is probable that wetlands will exist within the vicinity of Crystal Lake. As such, a site visit will be conducted to confirm these findings. If wetlands are identified in the area, it is likely that the wetlands will be considered intermediate resource value and require a 50-foot transition area (as found at N.J.A.C. 7:7A-3.3(d)). Should freshwater wetlands, transition areas, or State open waters be disturbed for this project, various General Permits will be required including but not limited to General Permit 11 – outfalls and intake structures, General Permit 17 – trails and boardwalks, General Permit 19 – docks and piers, General Permit 20 – bank stabilization, General Permit 21 – above ground utility lines, or General Permit 25 – minor channel or stream cleaning for local

The project will be required to comply with various requirements specific to each permit. Site Investigation Based on a preliminary desktop assessment of the environmental conditions revealed within the NJDEP’s GeoWeb online GIS resource and the USDA Web Soil Survey, freshwater wetlands do not appear to be located within the project area. Therefore, CME will conduct a site visit to confirm these findings, utilizing the methodologies outlined in the Federal Manual for Identifying and Delineating Jurisdictional Wetlands (1989), as required by the NJDEP under   the   Freshwater   Wetlands   Protection   Act   Rules.   If   wetlands   are   present,   the wetland/upland boundaries will be determined based upon field examination of topography, hydrology, soils, and vegetation characteristics at the site. Additionally, CME will also identify top of bank for regulated waters within the proposed work area to accurately represent the riparian zones associated with the State open waters adjacent to the site. Top of bank is typically characterized by an observable change or break in the slope of the land along a regulated water. In the event this break is not identifiable, other methods described in Flood Hazard Area Control Act Rules will be utilized to define top of bank. State Historic Preservation Office (SHPO) The project site is not mapped as a historic property, historic district, or historic archeological site according to the Departments NJ-GeoWeb online GIS resources. As such, SHPO comments are not anticipated while the wetlands application is being reviewed. This will be discussed during the pre-application meeting. With respect to the work on the pathway, this proposal assumes that the surface will not be HMA as this surface will be counted as impervious and could qualify the project as a Major Development for Stormwater Design. Further, the RFP lists the pathway width as 6 to 8 feet. Since Freshwater Wetlands General Permit #17 will be utilized for this work, and since the permit conditions restrict the maximum pathway width to 6 feet, the pathway work will not widen any part of the pathway to more than 6 feet. The project is not intended to qualify as a Major Development for Stormwater Design. Therefore, while not specifically required, GPI will include the replacement of inlet heads and grates as required by the stormwater management rules. There are additional items listed in the Alternate Scope of Work. These items are specifically discussed in Section I of this proposal. Pursuant to NJAC 7:13-2.3(b),   a flood hazard area exists along every regulated water that has a drainage area of 50 acres or greater. If a regulated water has a drainage area of less than 50 acres, the water does not possess a flood hazard area that is regulated under this chapter .   Based upon the documentation attached to the RFP, and our independent review, the Crystal Lake has a drainage area of approximately 23 acres. Therefore, Flood Hazard Area permitting relating to flood hazard areas is not anticipated, or included in this proposal. documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING INTERLOCAL AGREEMENT WITH BOROUGH OF ROSELAND FOR ANIMAL CONTROL SERVICES - 2025

pages.text: 317-24  November 12, 2024  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   and   Borough   of   Roseland  ( “ Roseland ” )   have   previously   executed   Inter-Local   Agreements   whereby   the   Township   would  provide   Roseland   with   certain   animal   control   services   in   exchange   for   quarterly   payments   by  Roseland to the Township (the   “ Inter-Local Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   now   intends   to   renew   the   Inter-Local   Agreement,   for   a   two  (2)-year   term,   whereby   animal   control   services   shall   be   provided   to   Roseland   from   January   1,  202 5   to   Dec ember 31, 202 6   (the   “ Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS,   under   the   Agreement,   the   Borough   of   Roseland   will   pay   the   Township   of  West   Orange   the   sum   of   $ 18,925   per   annum   to   be   prorated   on   a   quarterly   basis   of   $ 4, 73 1.25   per  quarter   for   the   contract   year   20 2 3   and   the   sum   of   $ 19 , 872   per   annum   to   be   prorated   on   a  quarterly basis of $ 4968 . 00   per quarter for the contract year   202 4 ; and  WHEREAS,   this   Resolution   authorizes   that   twenty-five   (25%)   of   the   per   annum   amount  for   each   contract   year   be   dedicated   to   and   placed   in   the   dog   license   account   t o   cover   the   cost   of  personnel and other expenses authorized under the   A greement ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   Health   Officer   has   reviewed   the   Agreement   and  recommends execution of the terms contained therein; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   Roseland   Resolution   and   as   stated   in   the   Inter-Local  Agreement,   Roseland   has   agreed   to   indemnify   the   Township   for   any   services   provided   for   the  Borough, as stated in Schedule A attached to the Agreement;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   and   counter-sign   the

Agreement   with   the   Borough   of   Roseland   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   and   the   Municipal  Clerk be and is hereby authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature thereon ;  RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   award   shall   be   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with any applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   November 12, 2024

1  4848-4827-0498, v. 1  2879-24  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   CHAPTER   3 ,   S E CTION   1 7 . 1   OF   THE   REVISED   GENERAL  ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ( ORDINANCE   AND RESOLUTION   - Preparation )  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   MUNICIPAL   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST  ORANGE,   NEW   JERSEY   that   Chapter   3   o f   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the   Township   of   West  Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The   purpose   of   this   ordinance   is   to   require   that   ordinances   which   revise   or   amend   provisions   of  the   West   Orange   Municipal   Code   be   drafted   in   a   manner   to   show   the   changes   being   made   to   the   Code  under the Ordinance.  I I .   CHAPTER   3 ,   SECTION   1 7 . 1   SHALL   BE   AND   HEREBY   IS   AMENDED   AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING:  3 - 1 7 . 1   Preparation .  Ordinances   and   resolutions   shall   be   prepared   for   the   consideration   of   Township   Council   by   the   Township  Attorney   or   the   legal   assistant   upon   motion   of   the   Council   or   request   of   the   Mayor.   Prior   to   introduction,  each   proposed   ordinance   requiring   or   permitting   administrative   action   shall   be   submitted   to   the   Business  Administrator   and   the   department   head   concerned.   Ordinance s   which   revise   or   amend   provisions   of   the  West   Orange   Municipal   Code   shall   be   drafted   in   a   manner   showing   the   revisions   or   amendments   being  made to the Code.  I II .   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   which   are   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   hereby   repealed   to  the extent of such conflict.  I V .   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   parts   shall   be   severed   and   th e  invalidity thereof shall not a ffect the remaining parts of this Ordinance.  V I .   EFFECTIVE DATE  This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in accordance with the law.  SUSAN McCARTNEY,   MAYOR  BILL RUTHERFORD ,  COUNCIL PRESIDENT  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C., MUNICIPAL CLERK

2  4848-4827-0498, v. 1  INTRODUCED:   October 29,   2024  ADOPTED:   N ovember 12, 2024  2879-24  Legislative History  The   purpose   of   this   ordinance   is   to   require   that   ordinances   which   revise   or   amend  provisions   of   the   West   Orange   Municipal   Code   be   drafted   in   a   manner   to   show   the   changes   being  made to the Code under the Ordinance .

Subsection 7-7.3 Temporary Parking Prohibition for Snow Plowi ng and Removal,  of the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and  amended as follows: ( italic type denotes deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  Name of Street   Side   Days   Location  Oxford Terrace   West   All   Entire Length  Section 2.   Subsection 7-14.1c Night Parking Restricted,   of the Revised General Ordinances  of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as follows: ( italic type denotes  deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  EXCEPTIONS  Name of Street   Side   Location  Oxford Terrace   Both   Entire Length  Section 3.   Subsection 7-1 2 Parking Prohibited for Street Cleaning and Maintenance , of the  Revised General Ordinances of the Township of West Orange is hereby supplemented and amended as  follows: ( italic type denotes deletion,   bold type denotes addition):  Name of Street   Sides   Hours and Days   Location  Oxford Terrace   East   Mondays 9AM-12PM   Entire Length  Oxford Terrace   West   Thursdays 9AM-12PM   Entire Length  Sectio n 4.   If any provisions of this Ordinance or application thereof, under any  circumstances, is held invalid, the invalidity shall not affect any of the provisions or applications of this  Ordinance that can be given effect without the valid provision(s) or application and to this end, the  provisions of this Ordinance are severable.

All other Ordinances or parts of Ordinances inconsistent with this Ordinance are hereby repealed.  Susan McCartney, Mayor   Bill Rutherford, Council President  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk  Introduced:   November 12 , 2024  Adopted:   November 25 , 2024

2880-24  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   creates   an   opportunity   for   overnight   parking   on   Oxford   Terrace,   where   residents  need   on - street   parking   in   addition   to   their   private   driveways.   In   addition,   the   ordinance   requires   the  residents   to   move   those   vehicles   as   needed   for   the   purpose   of   snow   plowing,   street   sweeping   and   other  maintenance at certain times.

#14088282.2  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ORDINANCE NUMBER   2881-24  ORDINANCE   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE  COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY   PROVIDING   FOR  A   SPECIAL   EMERGENCY   APPROPRIATION   IN   AN   AMOUNT  NOT   TO   EXCEED   $1, 8 00,000   TO   FUND   CONTRACTUALLY  REQUIRED   SEVERANCE   LIABILITIES   RESULTING   FROM   THE  RETIREMENT OF EMPLOYEES   IN THE   TOWNSHIP  WHEREAS ,   N.J.S.A.   40A:4-53 h   provides   that   a   local   unit   such   as   the   Township   of   West  Orange,   in   the   County   of   Essex,   State   of   New   Jersey   (the   “ Township ” ),   may   adopt   an   ordinance  authorizing   a   special   emergency   appropriation   for   the   payoff   of   contractually   required   severance  liabilities resulting from the layoff or retirement of employees of the Township; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:4-53 h ,   the   Township   desires   to   authorize   a   special  emergency   appropriation   in   the   amount   of   $1, 8 00,000   to   fund   the   cost   of   contractually   required  severance liabilities resulting from the retirement of employees of the Township .  BE   IT   ORDAINED   AND   ENACTED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF   ESSEX,   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY   (not  less than the majority of all members thereof affirmatively concurring) AS FOLLOWS:  SECTION   1 .   Pursuant   to   N.J.S. A.   40A:4-53 h ,   the   amount   of   $1, 8 00,000   (i)   is  hereby   appropriated   to   fund   the   cost   of   contractually   required   severance   liabilities   resulting   from  the   retirement   of   employees   of   the   Township ,   and   (ii)   shall   be   deemed   a   special   emergency  appropriation as defined and provided for in N.J.S. A.   40A:4-5 3 h .  SECTION   2 .   To   finance   the   appropriation   authorized   hereunder,   and   describ ed   in  Section   1   hereof,   special   emergency   notes   of   the   Township   (the   “ Notes ” )   shall   be   authorized   by   a  resolution   of   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   to   be   adopted   in   accordance   with   the  provisions of N.J.S.A. 40A:4-55.  SECTION   3 .   A   certified   copy   of   this   ordinance   as   finally   adopted   will   be   filed   with  the   Director   of   the   Division   of   Local   Government   Services,   in   the   New   Jersey   Department   of  Community Affairs.  SECTION   4 .   This   ordinance   shall   take   effect   twenty   (20)   days   after   final   adoption  and ap proval by the Mayor.  ADOPTED ON FIRST READING  DATED:   November 12, 2024  _____________________________________  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C.   BILL   RUTHERFORD , Council President  Municipal Clerk

#14088282.2  CLERK ’ S CERTIFICATE  I,   KAREN   J.   CARNEVALE,   R.M.C.,   DO   HEREBY   CERTIFY   that   I   am   the   Clerk   of  the   Township   of   West   Orange,   in   the   County   of   Essex   (the   “ Township ” ),   a   municipal   corporation  organized   and   existing   under   the   laws   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   and   that   as   such   I   am   duly  authorized   to   execute   and   deliver   this   certificate   on   behalf   of   the   Township.   In   such   capacity,   I  have   the   responsibility   to   maintain   the   minutes   of   the   meetings   of   the   governing   body   of   the  Township   and   the   records   relative   to   all   resolutions   and   ordinances   of   the   Township.   The  representations   made   herein   are   based   upon   the   records   of   t he   Township.   I   DO   HEREBY  FURTHER CERTIFY THAT:  ( 1 )   Attached   hereto   is   the   ordinance   introduced   on   November   12 ,   202 4 ,  finally adopted on   November   2 5 ,   202 4   and approved by the Mayor on   November   2 5 ,   202 4 . Copies   of   the   ordinance   were  made available to all who requested s ame. ( 5 )   The   ordinance   has   not   been   amended,   added   to,   altered   or   repealed  and said ordinance is now in full force and effect.  ( 6 )   A   certified   copy   of   the   ordinance   has   been   filed   with   the   Director   of   the  Division of Local Government Services, New Jersey Department of Community Affairs.  ( 7 )   The   official   seal   of   the   Township   is   the   seal,   an   impression   of   which   is  affixed, imprinted or reproduced opposite my signature on this Certificate.  IN   WITNESS   WHEREOF ,   I   have   hereunto   set   my   hand   and   affixed,   imprinted   or  reproduced the official seal of the Township   this   XX day of XXXX 2024 .  (SEAL)  KAREN J. pages.text: 317-24  November   25 , 2024  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   and   Borough   of   Roseland  ( “ Roseland ” )   have   previously   executed   Inter-Local   Agreements   whereby   the   Township   would  provide   Roseland   with   certain   animal   control   services   in   exchange   for   quarterly   payments   by  Roseland to the Township (the   “ Inter-Local Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   now   intends   to   renew   the   Inter-Local   Agreement,   for   a   two  (2)-year   term,   whereby   animal   control   services   shall   be   provided   to   Roseland   from   January   1,  202 5   to   December 31, 202 6   (the   “ Agreement ” ); and  WHEREAS,   under   the   Agreement,   the   Borough   of   Roseland   will   pay   the   Township   of  West   Orange   the   sum   of   $ 18,925   per   annum   to   be   prorated   on   a   quarterly   basis   of   $ 4, 73 1.25   per  quarter   for   the   contract   year   20 2 3   and   the   sum   of   $ 19 , 872   per   annum   to   be   prorated   on   a  quarterly basis of $ 4968 . 00   per quarter for the contract year   202 4 ; and  WHEREAS,   this   Resolution   authorizes   that   twenty-five   (25%)   of   the   per   annum   amount  for   each   contract   year   be   dedicated   to   and   placed   in   the   dog   license   account   t o   cover   the   cost   of  personnel and other expenses authorized under the   A greement ; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   Health   Officer   has   reviewed   the   Agreement   and  recommends execution of the terms contained therein; and  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   the   Roseland   Resolution   and   as   stated   in   the   Inter-Local  Agreement,   Roseland   has   agreed   to   indemnify   the   Township   for   any   services   provided   for   the  Borough, as stated in Schedule A attached to the Agreement;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   To wnship   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   and   counter-sign   the

Agreement   with   the   Borough   of   Roseland   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   and   the   Municipal  Clerk be and is hereby authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature thereon ;  RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   award   shall   be   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with any applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   November   25 , 2024

318-24  November 25, 2024  R E S O L U T I O N  WHEREAS ,   on   September   2 3 rd   202 4 ,   the   Township ’ s   Construction   Supervisor   inspected  Beverly   Road   and   Carter   Road   as   part   of   PSE&G   Phase   3   milling   and   paving   for   the   FY   2024   and   as   a  result,   it   was   concluded   that   Carter   Road   and   Beverly   Road   needed   curb   replacements   along   with  driveway repairs,   storm sewer   pipe, manholes   and   inlets   repairs;   and  WHEREAS,   the   inspection   revealed   that   the   condition   of   the   curb   at   Carter   Road   and   Beverly  Road   was   in   deplorable   condition   with   multiple   blocks   loose   in   the   street   and   without   a   proper   curb  footing ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   granite   block   curb   will   fall   once   PSE&G   paving   Contractor   was   milled   the  roads and prohibiting paving operations; and  WHEREAS ,   storm   sewers   structures   needed   immediate   repair   to   sustain   the   weight   of   the  equipment necessary for milling   and paving operations ; and  WHEREAS , pursuant to the Local Public Contracts Law, Emergency Contracts, N.J.S.A.  40A:11.6a the   Municipal Engineer on   October 8 th ,   202 4   notified the   Administration and  Purchasing Agent of the need to perform an emergency contract for this work; and  WHEREAS,   it   was   concluded that to expedite the work and to perform the emergency   repair of  the   inlets and manholes on the   roadway s   to protect public health and safety ,   an emergency contract w as  issued to   S Brothers ,   Inc.   who was readily available to mobilize and perform the work ; and  WHEREAS,   S Brothers , Inc.   submitted   an invoice   dated   October 30 , 202 4   for the   emergency  repair   of the granite block curb, pipe,   various inlets and manholes   and   its associated improvements   in   the  amount of   $ 334,645.73 ; and  WHEREAS , the   Municipal   Engineer by letter dated   November 14 , 202 4   recommends paying the  costs of this emergency work; and

WHEREAS , sufficient funds exist for this work; and  WHEREAS , the Township Council   concurs in the foregoing .  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West  Orange that an emergency appropriation in   an   amount   not to exceed $ $334,645.73   without   authorization  is authorized   to   S Brothers , Inc.   for emergency   work to repair   curb, associated driveways, storm pipe and  various inlets and manholes .  BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that this work was performed pursuant to the provisions of the  Local Public   C ontracts Law, Emergency Contracts ,   N.J.S.A. 40A:11-6 as the work was required   t o  protect public health and welfar e.  Karen J. Carnevale, RMC, Municipal Clerk   Bill   Rutherford , Council President  I   certify funds are available for this work   Account No.   03-2830-23-0500-010  $ 334,645.73  John   C. 320-24  November 25, 2024  R E   S   O   L   U   T   I   O   N  WHEREAS ,   o n   September 30 th ,   2024 , the   Department of Engineering and   DPW   Construction  Inspector James Williams inspected a storm sewer manhole at Wellington Avenue after the CCTV  inspection and subsurface investigation were completed and it was concluded that the storm manhole was  compromised and obstructing the flow of   stormwater ;   and  WHEREAS ,   the Municipal Engineer proceeded to contract   Grabowski Construction ., Inc. to  conduct   the necessary repairs to the storm sewer manhole   along Wellington Avenue; and  WHEREAS , pursuant to the Local Public Contracts Law, Emergency Contracts, N.J.S.A.  40A:11.6a   the   Municipal   Engineer   on   October   30 ,   2024   notified   the   Administration   and   Purchasing  Agent  of the need to perform an emergency contract for this work; and  WHEREAS,   it was concluded that to expedite th is   work to protect public health and safety, an  emergency   contract   was   issued   to   Grabowski   Construction .,   Inc.   who   was   readily   available   to   mobilize  and  perform the work; and  WHEREAS , the Municipal Engineer by letter dated   November 14 , 2024 recommends paying the  costs of this emergency work; and  WHEREAS,   Grabowski Construction., Inc   submitted invoice dated   November 4 th ,   2024 for  the emergency storm sewer   repair   in the amount of $ 7,200 .00 ; and  WHEREAS , sufficient funds   exist for this work; and  WHEREAS , the Township Council concurs in the foregoing .  NOW THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED   by the Township Council of the Township of West  Orange that an emergency appropriation in the amount   not to exceed   $ 7,200 .00   is authorized   to  Grabowski Construction.,   Inc.   for emergency   s torm   sewer   repair   along   Wellington Avenue.

BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED   that this work was performed pursuant to the provisions of the  Local Public   C ontracts Law, Emergency Contracts ,   N.J.S.A. 40A:11-6 as the work was required   t o  protect public health and welfar e.  Karen J. RESOLUTION GRANTING APPLICATION FOR LOCAL LICENSE TO OPERATE A CANNABIS CULTIVATION BUSINESS - YERRR CANNA 4872 - 9796 - 6836 V.1

pages.text: 321-24  November 25, 2024  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40:48-2   and   N.J.S.A.   24:61-45,   the   Township   Council  of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   adopted   Ordinance   26 47 -21   on   July   13,   2021,  amending the Township Code to   establish   rules and   regulations governing cannabis; and  WHEREAS,   the   Township ’ s   rules   and   regulations   governing   cannabis   within   the  Township   includes   a   local   license   requirement,   which   requires   proposed   cannabis   businesses   to  apply   for   and   obtain   the   Township   Council ’ s   approval   for   the   issuance   of   a   local   license   to  operate a cannabis business within the Township of West Orange (a   “ Local License ” ); and  WHEREAS,   Yerrr   Canna ,   LLC ,   an   entity   registered   to   do   business   in   the   State   of   New  Jersey,   submitted   a n   application   for   a   Local   License   for   the   proposed   operation   of   a   cannabis  cultivation   business   within   the   Township   at   the   property   located   at:   Block   62 ,   Lot   22.01,  commonly known as   82   Ashland   Avenue   ( “ Application ” ); and  WHEREAS,   Yerrr   Canna,   LLC ,   has   received   site   plan   approval   from   the   Township ’ s  Planning Board for the above-referenced proposed cannabis   cultivation   business; and  WHEREAS,   Yerrr   Canna,   LLC ,   received   final   approval   from   the   State   of   New   Jersey  Cannabis   Regulatory   Commission   ( “ CRC ” )   for   a   Class   I   Micro   Cannabis   Cultivation   Annual  License ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   issuance   of   a   Local   License   to   Yerrr   Canna,   LLC ,   to   operate   as   a  Cannabis   Cultivation   Business   will   not   exceed   the   Township's   current   limit   of   cannabis  cultivation   businesses;

2  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange ,   County   of   Essex ,   State   of   New   Jersey,   in   consideration   of   the   recommendations  of the   Chief of Police   hereby determines to be in the best interests of the Township, as follows:  1.   The above recitals are incorporated as though set forth at length herein.  2.   The   application   of   Yerrr   Canna,   LLC ,   for   a   local   license   to   operate   a   cannabis  cultivation   business   within   the   Township   of   West   Orange   at   the   prope rty   located   at:   Block   62 ,  Lot   22 .01   (commonly   known   as   82   Ashland   Avenue ,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey),   is   in  compliance   with   the   Township's   local   regulations   on   cannabis   businesses   and   is   hereby  approved.  3.   This   Resolution   shall   constitute   proof   that   said   application   complies   with   the  Township's local restrictions pursuant to   N.J.S.A.   24:6I-45(c) (1) .  4.   The   issuance   of   a   Local   License   to   operate   a   cannabis   cultivation   business   within  the Township   would not exceed the Township's current limit of   cannabis cultivation   business es .  5 .   T he   Township   Clerk   is   authorized   and   directed   to   issue   a   Local   License   to   Yerrr  Canna, LLC .  6 .   The   Township   Clerk   shall   also   promptly   provide   Yerrr   Canna,   LLC ,   and   the   New  Jersey Cannabis Regulatory Commission with a certified copy of this Resolution.  7 .   This Resolution shall take effect immediately.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford ,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Ado pted: November 25, 2024

documents.title: RESOLUTION GRANTING WEST ORANGE WELLNESS - RENEWAL OF LOCAL LICENSE TO OPERATE A CANNABIS RETAIL BUSINESS

pages.text: 322-24  November 25,   2024  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   on   November   20,   2023,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   adopted   Resolution  386-23   granting   a   local   license   to   operate   a   Cannabis   Retail   Business   to   West   Orange   Wellness  LLC   operating   at   the   property   located   at:   Block   4,   Lot   12,   commonly   known   as   26   So.   Valley  Road, West Orange, New Jersey   (the   “ Location ” ) ; and  WHEREAS,   the   local   license   granted   to   West   Orange   Wellness,   LLC   was   for   a   term   of  one (1) year; and  WHEREAS,   West   Orange   Wellness,   LLC   submitted   a   renewal   application   for   its   local  license   to   the   Office   of   the   Township   Clerk   seeking   an   additional   one   (1)   year   license   to   operate  a cannabis retail business within the Township ; and  WHEREAS,   West   Orange   Wellness,   LLC   has   not   begun   commencement   of   its   retail  operations as they are continuing improvements at the Location ; and  WHEREAS,   West   Orange   Wellness   LLC,   has   advised   that   there   are   no   changes   in   its  renewal application from its initial application for a local license.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange ,   County   of   Essex ,   State   of   New   Jersey,   hereby   determines   to   be   in   the   best  interests of the Township, as follows:  1.   The above recitals are incorporated as though set forth at length herein.  2.   The   renewal   application   of   West   Orange   Wellness ,   LLC   for   a   local   license   to  operate   a   cannabis   retail   business   within   the   Township   at   the   prope rty   located   at:   Block   4 ,   Lot   12  (commonly   known   as   26   So.   Valley   Road,   West   Orange,   New   Jersey),   is   in   compliance   with   the  Township's local regulations on cannabis businesses,   a nd is hereby approved.

2  3.   West   Orange   Wellness,   LLC ’ s   Renewal   License   shall   be   effective   as   of  November 20, 2024 consistent with the date of its initial local license .  4 .   The   Township   Clerk   shall   promptly   provide   West   Orange   Wellness ,   LLC   and   the  New Jersey Cannabis Regulatory Commission with a certified copy of this Resolution.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford ,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Ado pted: Nov ember 25, 2024

1  323-24  November 25, 2024  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   Council   for   the   Township   of   West   Orange   pursuant   to  N.J.S.A.   40A:12-13-1   authorizes   the   Township   to   sell   the   undersized   lot   known   as   Block   175.14,  Lot 2 on the Township of West Orange Tax Map (the   “ Property ” ); and  WHEREAS,   on   October   16,   2024 ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:12-13.2,   the   Township  issued   letters   to   the   owners   of   the   properties   contiguous   to   the   Property   (the   “ Contiguous  Owners ” )   providing   notice   that   the   Township   intended   to   sell   the   Property   and   that   the   Township  was providing the Contiguous Owners, the right to prior refusal to purchase the Property ; and  WHEREAS,   on   October   17 ,   2024 ,   Richard   Calderon   and   Chautuyen   Tran   (the  “ Buyers ” ),   the   owners   of   57   Mellon   Avenue,   submitted   a   formal   offer   consistent   with   the  provisions   of   the   proposed   Agreement   of   Sale   (the   “ Agreement ” ),   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A ”  and  WHEREAS,   the   Agreement   provides   that   the   Buyers   will   pay   to   the   Township   $ 7, 500  for   the   purchase   of   the   Property   which   is   consistent   with   the   valuation   provided   by   Mark  Hendricks, CRE, FRICS, CTA, SCGREA ;   and  WHEREAS,   the   Agreement   provides   that   the   closing   for   the   purchase   of   the   Property  shall   occur   no   later   than   fourteen   (14)   days   from   the   authorization   of   the   sale   by   adoption   of   this  resolution; and  WHEREAS,   the   Agreement   provides   that   the   Buyers   are   obligated   to   obtain   a   survey   of  the   Property ;   and   to   prepare   the   deed   including   the   required   deed   restriction   for   the   closing   as  well as any other documents required for the Buyers to obtain title insurance; and  WH EREAS,   the Buyers have already obtained the required survey .

2  4814-7369-3346, v. 1  THEREFORE,   IT   IS   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   that   the   Township   Council   authorizes  the   Township   to   enter   into   the   Agreement   with   the   Buyers,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”  pursuant to the terms and conditions set forth therein ;   and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED,   that   the   Mayor   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   the  Agreement   with   the   Buyers,   annexed   hereto   as   Exhibit   “ A, ”   and   the   Township   Clerk   is  authorized to attest to the Mayor ’ s signature;   and  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVE D   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   and   exhibit   hereto   shall  r emain on file with the   Township   Clerk .  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Bill Rutherford ,  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: November 25, 2024

PROPOSED  A GREEMENT OF SALE  THIS   AGREEMENT   OF   SALE   (this   “ Agreement ” ) ,   made   as   of   the   25 th   day   of  Novembe r,   2024   by   and   between   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   ( the   “ Seller ”   or   the  “ Township ” )   and   RICHARD CALDERON and CHAUTUYEN TRAN   (the   “ Buyer ” ).  W I T N E S S E T H :  WHEREAS ,   the   Seller   is   the   owner   of   certain   real   propert y   known   as   Block   1 75. 14 ,   Lot  2   on   the   Township   of   West   Orange   Tax   Map   (the   “ Property ” ),   an   undeveloped   and   undersized  lot adjacent to the property commonly known as   ____   Mellon Avenue ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   November   25,   2024,   the   Township   Council   for   the   Township   of   West  Orange   adopted   resolution   323-24   authorizing   the   Township   to   sell   the   Property   pursuant   to  N.J.S.A.   40A:12-13.2 ; and  WHEREAS ,   on   October   16,   2024 ,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:12-13.2,   the   Township  issued   a   letter   to   the   owners   of   the   properties   contiguous   to   the   Property   (the   “ Contiguous  Owners ” )   providing   notice   that   the   Township   intended   to   sell   the   Property   and   that   the   Township  was providing the Contiguous Owners, the right to prior refusal to purchase the Property; and  WHEREAS ,   on   ,   the   Buyer   submitted   a   formal   offer   consistent   with  the   provisions of this Agreement.  NOW,   THEREFORE,   for   and   in   consideration   of   the   covenants   and   agreements  hereinafter   set   forth   and   other   good   and   valuable   consideration,   the   receipt   and   sufficiency   of  which is hereby acknowledged, and the mutual promises herein made, it is agreed as follows:  1.   SALE .   Seller   agree s   to   sell   and   Buyer   agree s   to   purchase   the   Seller ’ s   right,  title ,   and   interest   in   and   to   the   Property   for   the   Purchase   Price   (as   hereinafter   defined)   and   upon  the terms and conditions hereinafter p rovided.

2  4851-0739-6757, v. 1  2.   PURCHASE PRICE .   The   Purchase   Price   of   the   Property   is   SEVEN  THOUSAND   FIVE   HUNDRED   AND 00/ 100   ($ 7,5 00 )   DOLLARS   (the   “ Purchase Price ” ) .  The Purchase Price shall be payable as follows:  A.   Upon   execution   of   th is   Agreement,   the   Buyer   shall   pay   the   entire   Purchase  Price , to be held in escrow by the   Township ,   until the Closing (as hereinafter defined).  B.   A t   the   Closing   Date   as   defined   herein ,   the   Township   shall   release   the  Purchase Price from escrow and accept the entire Purchase Price.  3.   CLOSING DOCUMENTS .  A.   At   the   Closing,   Seller   shall   execute   and/or   deliver   to   the   Buyer :   (i)   a   D eed  (the   “ Deed ” ),   in   proper   form   for   recording;   and   (ii)   such   other   instruments   as   may   be   reasonably  required   by   Buyer ’ s   attorney   or   Buyer ’ s   title   insurance   company   to   effectuate   the   within  transaction , including an Affidavit of Title .  B.   At   the   Closing,   Buyer   shall   execute   and/or   deliver   to   Seller   such   other  documents   and/or   instruments   as   may   be   reasonably   required   by   Seller ’ s   attorney s   or   Buyer ’ s  title insurance company to effectuate   the within transaction.  4.   TITLE .   The   dimensions   of   the   Property   shall   be   set   forth   on   a   survey  which   may   be   ordered   by   the   Buyer   at   the   Buyer ’ s   expense.   The   sale   is   subject   to   easements   and  restrictions   of   record,   if   any,   and   such   state   of   facts   as   an   accurate   survey   might   disclose ,  provided   same   do   not   render   title   uninsurable   at   regular   rates .   The   sale   is   also   subject   to  applicable   zoning   ordinances.   The   Seller   shall   provide   title,   free   and   clear   of   all   mortgages,  encumbrances and liens.  5.   POSSESSION .   At   the   Closing,   Seller   shall   deliver   possession   of   the  Property to the Buyer .

3  4851-0739-6757, v. 1  6.   DUE DILIGENCE .   The   Buyer   shall   be   solely   responsible   for   the   investigation  of   all   facts   and   data   which   it   has   deemed   necessary   or   desirable   to   enter   into   this   Agreement  (referred   to   as   “ Due   Diligence ” ).   Due   Diligence   shall   also   include   any   and   all   investigations  regarding   title   and   investigations   regarding   any   and   all   impediments   related   to   title.   Buyer  consents   and   acknowledges   that   a ll   Due   Diligence   has   been   completed   or   shall   be   completed   by  the Closing Date as defined herein .  7.   CONDITION OF PROPERTY .   Buyer   acknowledge s   and   agree s   that   it   ha s  the   opportunity   to   inspect   the   Property   during   the   Due   Diligence   including,   but   not   limited   to,   all  of   the   improvements   and   the   land   comprising   the   Property.   The   Buyer   acknowledge s   that   it   is  purchasing   the   Property   “ WHERE   IS ”   and   “ AS   IS ”   without   reliance   upon   any   warranty   or  representation   made   by   Seller   or   by   any   of   i t s   agents   or   representatives ,   including   any   broker,   of  any   kind   or   nat ure ,   except   that   the   Seller   has   not   received   any   notice   regarding   any  environmental   issue   affecting   the   Property.   The   Buyer   shall   be   responsible,   at   its   sole   cost   and  expense :   (i)   to   obtain   whatever   governmental   certificates   or   approvals ,   that   might   be   necessary   to  convey   the   Property   to   the   Buyer ;   and   (ii)   to   conduct   an   inspection   and   perform   all   Due  Diligence   of   the   Property.   Buyer   understands   and   agrees   to   accept   the   Property   in   its   current  undeveloped   state .   Notwithstanding   the   above,   Seller   will   con vey   to   Buyer   good   and   marketable  title   to   the   Property,   insurable   at   regular   rates   by   a   title   insurance   company   licensed   to   do  business in   New Jersey , chosen by   Buyer ,   subject to the restrictions set forth herein .  8.   CONDITIONS   OF   SALE .   Buyer   agrees   that   a   Deed   of   Merger   or   Consolidation  will   be   recorded   with   the   County   contemporaneously   with   the   Closing.   Buyer   agrees   that   the  Deed   will   contain   a   restriction   in   perpetuity   that   no   residence   or   other   structure   other   than   a

4  4851-0739-6757, v. 1  garage   will   be   added   to   the   real   p roperty   in   accord a n ce   with   zoning   laws   and   other   applicable  laws.  9.   CLOSING   OF   TITLE .   T he   Closing   shall   take   place   within   fourteen   ( 14 )   days  after   the   later   of   the :   (i)   Township   Council   for   the   Township ’ s   approval   of   the   sale,   and   (ii)  approval   of   the   S ubdivision   by   the   Township   Planning   Board,   TIME   BEING   OF   THE  ESSENCE   (the   “ Closing   Date ” ),   at   the   offices   of   Trenk   Isabel   Siddiqi   &   Shahdanian   P.C. ,   290  W.   Mt.   Pleasant   Avenue,   Suite   2370,   Livingston,   Roseland,   New   Jersey   070 39 ,   or   at   such   other  location as ma y be mutually agreed by the parties, at a time mutu ally convenient for the parties.  10.   TOWNSHIP COUN CI L   APPROVAL .   The   parties '   obligations   set   forth   in  this   Agreement   are   expressly   subject   to   final   approval   by   the   Township   Council   for   the  Township   of   West   Orange   ( the   “ Township   Council ” )   to   be   manifested   in   the   adoption   of   a  resolution authorizing the sale of the Property   and   the execution of this Agreement.  11.   ENTIRE AGREEMENT .   This   Agreement   constitutes   the   entire   agreement  between   the   parties.   If   any   of   t he   terms   in   this   Agreement   conflict   with   any   previous   agreement  or   contract   between   the   parties,   the   terms   of   this   Agreement   prevail.   No   variations   or  modifications   of   or   amendments   to   the   terms   of   this   Agreement   shall   be   binding   unless   reduced  to writing and signed by the parties hereto.  12.   BINDING EFFECT .   This   Agreement   shall   be   binding   on   and   shall   inure   to   the  benefit of Seller and Buyer and their respective successors and assigns.  13.   CONSTRUCTION .   The   laws   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey   shall   govern   the  interp retation, construction and performance of this Agreement.  14.   FURTHER ASSURANCES .   Each   party   agrees   that   at   any   time   or   from  time   to   time   upon   written   request   of   the   other   party,   they   will   execute   and   deliver   all   such   further

5  4851-0739-6757, v. 1  documents   and   do   all   such   other   acts   and   things   as   may   be   reasonably   required   to   confirm   or  consummate this transaction.  15.   CAPTIONS .   The   captions   preceding   the   paragraphs   of   this   Agreement   are  intended   only   as   a   matter   of   convenience   for   reference   and   in   no   way   define,   limit   or   describe   th e  scope of this Agreement or the intent of any provision hereof.  16.   NOTICES .   All   notices   required   to   be   given   pursuant   to   this   Agreement   shall  be   sent   by   certified   mail,   return   receipt   requested,   or   overnight   courier   postage   prepaid,   return  receipt requested, or delivered personally at the addresses listed below for each party.  (a)   In the case of Township of West Orange :  Mayor   Susan McCartney  Township of West Orange  66 Main Street  West Orange, New Jersey 07052  Municipal Clerk  Towns hip of West Orange  66 Main Street  West Orange, New Jersey 07052  With copy to:  Richard D. Trenk, Esq.  Trenk Isabel Siddiqi & Shahdanian P.C.  290 W. Mt. Pleasant Avenue, Su ite 2370  Livingston, New Jersey 07039  (b)   In the case of the   [BUYER ’ S NAME] :  [BUYER ’ S NAME]  [BUYER ’ S ADDRESS]  With copy to:  [BUYER ’ S COUNSEL ’ S NAME]  [BUYER ’ S COUNSEL ’ S ADDRESS]  Any   party may change the notice address by written notice to the other party.

6  4851-0739-6757, v. 1  17.   REMEDIES .   If   Buyer   default s   in   its   obligations   hereunder,   Buyer   shall   waive  any   and   all   rights   to   the   funds   held   by   the   Township   in   escrow   as   liquidated   damages   and   S eller  shall   keep   the   funds   held   in   escrow   and   may   sell   the   Property   to   another   buyer.   If   the   Seller  defaults   in   i t s   obligations   hereunder   or   shall   fail   to   close   title   hereunder   for   any   reason,   then  Buyer,   as   its   sole   and   exclusive   remedy,   shall   be   solely   entitled   to   the   rescission   of   this  Agreement and return of   the   Purchase Price   paid to the Seller .  18.   WAIVER OF CONDITIONS .  A.   Buyer   and   Seller   each   shall   have   the   right   to   waive   any   of   the   terms   or  conditions   of   this   Agreement   which   are   strictly   for   their   respective   benefit   and   to   complete   the  Closing   in   accordance   with   the   terms   and   conditions   of   this   Agreement   which   have   not   been   so  waived.   Any   such   waiver   shall   be   effective   and   binding   only   if   made   in   writing   and   signed   by  the party who benefits from the condition being waived.  B.   No   waiver   by   either   party   of   any   failure   or   refusal   by   the   other   party   to  comply   with   their   obliga tions   hereunder   shall   be   deemed   a   waiver   of   any   other   or   subsequent  failure or refusal by the other party so to comply.  19.   SEVERABILITY .   The   terms,   conditions,   covenants   and   provisions   of   this  Agreement   shall   be   deemed   to   be   severable.   If   any   clause   or   provision   herein   contained   shall   be  determined   to   be   invalid   or   unenforceable   by   a   court   of   competent   jurisdiction   or   by   operation   of  applicable   law,   the   same   shall   be   deemed   to   be   severable   and   shall   not   affect   the   validity   of   any  other   clause   or   provision   of   this   Agreement   and   such   other   clauses   and   provisions   shall   remain   in  full   force   and   effect.   If,   however,   the   severed   clause   or   provision   relates   to   the   Purchase   Price   or  other   monies   to   be   paid   hereunder,   Seller   shall   have   the   right   to   terminate   this   Agreement   on  prior written   notice to the Buyer.

7  4851-0739-6757, v. 1  20.   GENDER .   As   used   in   this   Agreement,   the   masculine   gender   shall   include   the  feminine   or   neuter   genders,   the   neuter   gender   shall   include   the   masculine   or   feminine   genders,  the   singular   shall   include   the   plural   and   the   plural   shall   include   the   singular,   wherever  appropriate to the context.  21.   NO   BROKER .   Both   Seller   and   Buyer   represent   and   w ar rant   that   no   real  estate   broker,   agent,   or   salesperson   has   been   involved   in   this   transaction.   T he   Buyer   and   Seller  shall each indemnify the other if a brokerage commission is sought .  22.   MISCELLANEOUS .  A.   Each   person   signing   this   Agreement   warrants   that   he/she   has   fully  authority   to   execute   this   Agreement   and   bind   the   party   on   whose   behalf   he/she   has   signed   to   the  terms hereof.  B.   S eller   acknowledges   that   Purchaser   may   purchase   the   Property   as   part   of  an   IRC   Section   1031   Tax   Deferred   Exchange   for   Purchaser's   benefit.   Seller   agrees   to   assist   and  cooperate   in   such   exchange   for   the   benefit   of   Purchaser   provided   Seller   shall   incur   no   liability,  cost   or   expense   and   will   execute   any   and   all   documents,   subject   to   the   reasonable   approval   of   its  counsel, as are reasonably necessary in connection with such exchange.  C.   This   Agreement   may   be   executed   in   two   or   more   counterparts,   and   when  all   co unterparts   have   been   executed,   each   executed   counterpart   will   have   the   force   and   effect   of  the   original.   Each   party   to   this   Agreement   may   sign   and   deliver   to   the   other   party(ies)   a  facsimile   copy   of   this   Agreement,   in   counterparts,   or   with   a   conformed   signature   of   the   Debtor  with   the   same   effect   as   if   each   party   had   signed   and   delivered   to   the   other   party(ies)   an   original  of the same document.
